        row 8 {r8c5, r8c7, r8c9}: r8c7, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r8c5=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r6c6, r7c6в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        r5c6=X в†’ r5c8, r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: r9c3=X в†’ r9c4, r9c7в‰ X
        col 7 {r6c7, r8c7, r9c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r8c7в‰ X
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r5c8, r5c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r8c7, r8c9, r9c7}: r8c7, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r8c9=X
        r8c9=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c8, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c6=r6c7], r6c6, r7c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c5, [r6c3=r7c6], r9c4, r9c3} (spine [r6c3=r7c6]вЂ”r9c4).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r8c5=r9c3], r8c7, r8c9, r9c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c6=r6c7], r6c6, r7c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {[r6c3=r7c6]}.
      в†’ color(r9c3) = color([r6c3=r7c6]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c9, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c9=r9c5], r6c7, r6c6, r7c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c9=r9c5], r6c6} and {r6c7, r7c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c9=r9c5]) = color(r6c6) = X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, [r5c9=r9c5]}: [r5c9=r9c5]=X в†’ r5c6, r5c8в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c6=X в†’ r6c3, r6c7в‰ X
              row 9 {[r5c9=r9c5], r9c3, r9c9}: [r5c9=r9c5]=X в†’ r9c3, r9c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r6c6=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              col 9 {[r5c9=r9c5], r8c9, r9c9}: [r5c9=r9c5]=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X
              box 9 {r8c8, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r8c8=X
              r8c3=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c8, [r5c9=r9c5], r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r5c8, [r5c9=r9c5]в‰ X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, [r5c9=r9c5]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) в‰  color(r9c5). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r9c5, r7c6}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r9c5} and {r5c9, r7c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r9c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c6=X в†’ r5c8, r5c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c5=X в†’ r9c3, r9c9в‰ X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r6c6, r7c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r5c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r5c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r8c9=X
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r5c8, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
              box 9 {r8c8, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c8в‰ X
              r6c7=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c9=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r5c6, r5c8в‰ X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r5c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r8c9, r9c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r5c9=X в†’ r6c7в‰ X

              row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c7) в‰  color(r9c9). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c7, r8c7, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r6c3, r8c7} and {r6c7, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r8c7) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c7в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c9}: r8c7=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
              box 9 {r8c7, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c7=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: r9c3, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r9c5=X
              col 9 {r5c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r5c9=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              r5c9=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c5=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c9}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c7, r8c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r5c8, r5c9в‰ X
              box 9 {r8c7, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c7, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X
              r9c9=X в†’ r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c7=X and r9c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r8c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r8c9вЂ”r9c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 6, row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r5c9=r8c7], r6c7, r9c5} = {r5c6, [r5c9=r8c7], r7c6}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r8c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c8, r6c7, r9c5, r7c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c5, r8c9вЂ”r9c7}:

  3.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r6c7], r6c3, r6c6, r7c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r8c3=r9c4], [r8c5=r9c3], r8c9, r9c8}. Contradiction.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).

  2.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c6=r6c7], r6c6, r7c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c6, [r5c6=r6c7], [r6c3=r7c6], r7c7} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”[r6c3=r7c6]).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r7c7). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c7], [r6c3=r7c6], [r6c6=r7c7], r7c9} (spine [r6c3=r7c6]вЂ”[r6c6=r7c7]).
      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c7]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9], r5c9, r9c9} (spine [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9]вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9], [r5c8=r9c9], [r6c6=r7c7]} (spine [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9]вЂ”[r5c8=r9c9]).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color([r6c6=r7c7]). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r5c8=r9c9], [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9], [r5c9=r6c6=r7c7], [r6c3=r7c6]} (spine [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9]вЂ”[r5c9=r6c6=r7c7]).
      в†’ color([r5c8=r9c9]) = color([r6c3=r7c6]). Identify.
  8.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c8=r6c3=r7c6=r9c9], r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 7, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r7c6} = {r8c3}.
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c7, r7c7, [r7c6=r8c3]}:
      Diagonals: {r6c3, r7c7} and {r6c7, [r7c6=r8c3]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r7c7) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c7в‰ X
        row 7 {[r7c6=r8c3], r7c7, r7c9}: r7c7=X в†’ [r7c6=r8c3], r7c9в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, [r7c6=r8c3], r9c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, [r7c6=r8c3]}: r6c6, [r7c6=r8c3]в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        box 7 {[r7c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3}: [r7c6=r8c3], r9c3в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
        box 9 {r7c7, r7c9, r9c9}: r7c7=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X
        r5c6=X в†’ r5c8, r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 9 {r5c9, r7c9, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color([r7c6=r8c3]) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
        row 7 {[r7c6=r8c3], r7c7, r7c9}: [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r7c7, r7c9в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, [r7c6=r8c3], r9c3}: [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, [r7c6=r8c3]}: [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r5c8, r5c9в‰ X
        box 7 {[r7c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3}: [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c7, r7c9, r9c9}: r7c7, r7c9в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.

  2.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c6=r6c7], r6c6, r7c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 7, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r7c6]} = {r8c3}.
      в†’ color([r6c3=r7c6]) = color(r8c3), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c8} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c7, r6c3, [r8c3=r9c8]}:
      Diagonals: {r5c8, r6c3} and {r6c7, [r8c3=r9c8]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r6c3) = X
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c8=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c7в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        r5c8=X в†’ [r8c3=r9c8]в‰ X (adjacent)
        r7c6=X в†’ r7c7, r7c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 9 {r7c7, r7c9, [r8c3=r9c8]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color([r8c3=r9c8]) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
        row 9 {[r8c3=r9c8], r9c1, r9c2}: [r8c3=r9c8]=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r5c8, r5c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c7, r7c9, [r8c3=r9c8]}: [r8c3=r9c8]=X в†’ r7c7, r7c9в‰ X
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c8, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        row 7 {r7c6, r7c7, r7c9}: r7c7, r7c9в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6=X and r7c6=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c6=r6c7], r6c6, r7c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c6, [r5c6=r6c7], [r6c3=r7c6], r7c7} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”[r6c3=r7c6]).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r7c7). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c7], [r6c3=r7c6], [r6c6=r7c7], r7c9} (spine [r6c3=r7c6]вЂ”[r6c6=r7c7]).
      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c7]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9], r5c8, r9c8} (spine [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9]вЂ”r5c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9], [r5c9=r9c8], [r6c6=r7c7]} (spine [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9]вЂ”[r5c9=r9c8]).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color([r6c6=r7c7]). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r5c9=r9c8], [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9], [r5c8=r6c6=r7c7], [r6c3=r7c6]} (spine [r5c6=r6c7=r7c9]вЂ”[r5c8=r6c6=r7c7]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r9c8]) = color([r6c3=r7c6]). Identify.
  8.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c9=r6c3=r7c6=r9c8], r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  1.  SET: {row 7, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.

      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).

      Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c6]} = {r7c3}.
      в†’ color([r6c3=r8c6]) = color(r7c3), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c6=r6c7], r6c6, r9c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c6, [r5c6=r6c7], [r6c3=r9c6], r9c7} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”[r6c3=r9c6]).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c7], [r6c3=r9c6], [r6c6=r9c7], r9c1} (spine [r6c3=r9c6]вЂ”[r6c6=r9c7]).
      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c7]) = color(r9c1). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c6=r9c7], [r5c6=r6c7=r9c1], [r6c3=r9c6], r7c3} (spine [r5c6=r6c7=r9c1]вЂ”[r6c3=r9c6]).
      в†’ color([r6c6=r9c7]) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c6=r7c3=r9c7], r8c2, r8c8, r8c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c6} = {r7c3}.
      в†’ color(r9c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c7, r9c7, [r7c3=r9c6]}:
      Diagonals: {r6c3, r9c7} and {r6c7, [r7c3=r9c6]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r9c7) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c7в‰ X
        row 9 {[r7c3=r9c6], r9c1, r9c7}: r9c7=X в†’ [r7c3=r9c6], r9c1в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, [r7c3=r9c6]}: r6c6, [r7c3=r9c6]в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        box 7 {[r7c3=r9c6], r8c2, r9c1}: [r7c3=r9c6], r9c1в‰ X в†’ r8c2=X
        box 9 {r8c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r9c7=X в†’ r8c8, r8c9в‰ X
        r5c6=X в†’ r5c8, r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color([r7c3=r9c6]) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
        row 9 {[r7c3=r9c6], r9c1, r9c7}: [r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r9c1, r9c7в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, [r7c3=r9c6]}: [r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
        box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r5c8, r5c9в‰ X
        box 7 {[r7c3=r9c6], r8c2, r9c1}: [r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r8c2в‰ X
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c6=r6c7], r6c6, r9c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r9c6]} = {r7c3}.
      в†’ color([r6c3=r9c6]) = color(r7c3), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c6} = {r7c3}.
      в†’ color(r9c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r5c8 guards oddagon {r5c8, r5c6, [r7c3=r9c6], r6c3, r6c7} (source r6c6).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c8=r6c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c9, r6c7, r6c3, [r7c3=r9c6]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, [r6c7=r9c3]}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c3, [r6c7=r9c3]в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, [r6c7=r9c3], [r7c7=r8c3]}: r6c3, [r6c7=r9c3]в‰ X в†’ [r7c7=r8c3]=X
        box 9 {[r7c7=r8c3], r8c8, r8c9}: [r7c7=r8c3]=X в†’ r8c8, r8c9в‰ X
        r6c5=X в†’ r5c6, r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
        r7c6=X and [r7c7=r8c3]=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c7, r8c8, r8c9, r8c3} (spine r8c8вЂ”r8c9).
      в†’ color(r7c7) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c5, [r5c6=r6c7], r6c3, [r7c7=r8c3]} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color([r7c7=r8c3]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c6=r6c7], [r6c5=r7c7=r8c3], r7c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”[r6c5=r7c7=r8c3]).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c3=r7c6], [r6c5=r7c7=r8c3], r9c3, r9c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c5, r9c3:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c7, r7c7, r8c8}:
            Diagonals: {r5c8, r7c7} and {r6c7, r8c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r7c7) = X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c8=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r5c8=X в†’ r6c7в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r8c9}: r7c7=X в†’ r8c8, r8c9в‰ X
              r7c7=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c8, r8c9}: r8c8, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c3], r8c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r6c3, [r6c5=r9c3]в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c3], r6c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c3], r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r6c3, [r6c5=r9c3]в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c8, r8c9}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c3], r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) в‰  color(r9c3). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r6c5, r9c5, r7c6}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r9c5} and {r6c5, r7c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r9c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c6=X в†’ r5c8, r5c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7}: r5c8, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
              r5c6=X в†’ r6c5, r7c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c7=X в†’ r6c3, r7c7в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c5=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              r8c3=X в†’ r8c8, r8c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r8c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c3, r6c7в‰ X
              r6c5=X в†’ r5c6, r9c3, r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r7c6=X в†’ r7c7в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              r8c3=X в†’ r8c8, r8c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r8c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c7, r8c8, r8c9, r8c3} (spine r8c8вЂ”r8c9).
      в†’ color(r7c7) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c5, [r5c6=r6c7], r6c3, [r7c7=r8c3]} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color([r7c7=r8c3]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c5=r7c7=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r5c6=r6c7], r6c3, r9c3, r9c5, r7c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r8c7, [r5c6=r6c7], r9c7, r9c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r9c7).
      в†’ color(r8c7) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c5, r7c9, [r8c7=r9c6], r9c7} (spine r7c9вЂ”[r8c7=r9c6]).
      в†’ color(r7c5) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c3, [r5c6=r6c7], r6c5, [r7c5=r9c7]} (spine [r5c6=r6c7]вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color([r7c5=r9c7]). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c3=r7c5=r9c7], r8c3, [r8c7=r9c6], r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r8c3 guards oddagon {r8c3, r8c7, [r5c6=r6c7], r9c6, r9c2} (source r9c7).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c5, r7c9, [r8c3=r9c7], r9c6}:
      Diagonals: {r7c5, [r8c3=r9c7]} and {r7c9, r9c6} (non-adjacent).

      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r8c3 guards oddagon {r8c3, r8c7, [r5c6=r6c7], r9c6, r9c2} (source r9c7).

              box 9 {r7c9, [r8c3=r9c7], r8c7}: [r8c3=r9c7]=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X

  2.  Diamond {[r7c9=r8c5], r9c4, r9c5, r9c3} (spine r9c4вЂ”r9c5).
      в†’ color([r7c9=r8c5]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, r5c9, [r7c9=r8c5=r9c3]} (spine r5c3вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r7c9=r8c5=r9c3]). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r7c9=r8c5=r9c3], r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, r5c9вЂ”r6c9}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r7c9}.
      в†’ color(r9c3) = color(r7c9), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c6} and {r5c6, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c6) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, r5c9вЂ”r6c9}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r7c9, r7c6, r9c3, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r7c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c5, r7c6, [r7c9=r9c5], r9c3} (spine r7c6вЂ”[r7c9=r9c5]).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, r5c9вЂ”r6c9}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r7c9, r7c6, r9c3, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r7c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r7c9=r9c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r7c6, r8c5, r8c8, r9c8, r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, r5c9вЂ”r6c9}:

  2.  Diamond {r7c9, r7c6, r9c3, r9c4} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r7c9) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c5, r7c6, [r7c9=r9c4], r9c3} (spine r7c6вЂ”[r7c9=r9c4]).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r7c9=r9c4], [r8c5=r9c3], r8c8, r9c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, r5c9вЂ”r6c9}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r7c9, r7c6, r9c3, r9c4} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r7c9) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r7c9=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r7c6, r8c5, r8c8, r9c7, r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c5, r8c8, r8c9, r9c9} (spine r8c8вЂ”r8c9).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c9, [r8c5=r9c9], r8c9, r8c8} (spine [r8c5=r9c9]вЂ”r8c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c6, [r8c5=r9c9], r9c5, r9c3} (spine [r8c5=r9c9]вЂ”r9c5).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.

      в†’ color([r5c9=r8c8]) = color([r7c6=r9c3]). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c9=r7c6=r8c8=r9c3], r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color(r9c3) = color(r7c6), but they share a house. Contradiction.

      в†’ color(r9c3) = color(r7c6), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c9, r6c8, r6c3}:
      Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5, r9c4в‰ X
        r6c8=X в†’ r8c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c5, r8c8, r8c9}: r8c5, r8c8в‰ X в†’ r8c9=X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: r9c3, r9c4в‰ X в†’ r9c7=X
        box 9 {r8c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r8c9=X and r9c7=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c3) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r5c3, r5c6в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c8в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5, r9c4в‰ X
        r5c9=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c5, r8c8, r8c9}: r8c5, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r8c8=X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c7}: r9c3, r9c4в‰ X в†’ r9c7=X
        box 9 {r8c8, r8c9, r9c7}: r8c8=X and r9c7=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c5, r8c8, r8c9, r9c7} (spine r8c8вЂ”r8c9).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c6, [r8c5=r9c7], r9c4, r9c3} (spine [r8c5=r9c7]вЂ”r9c4).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c3, r5c6, [r7c6=r9c3]} (spine r5c3вЂ”r5c6).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color([r7c6=r9c3]). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c9=r7c6=r9c3], r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, r5c9вЂ”r6c8}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color(r9c3) = color(r7c6), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 8, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c3} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color(r9c3) = color(r7c6), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c9, r6c8, r6c3}:
      Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5, r9c4в‰ X
        r6c8=X в†’ r9c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c3) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r5c3, r5c6в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c8в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5, r9c4в‰ X
        r5c9=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c5, r8c7, r8c9}: r8c5, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r8c7=X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c8}: r9c3, r9c4в‰ X в†’ r9c8=X
        box 9 {r8c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r8c7=X and r9c8=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c5, r8c7, r8c9, r9c8} (spine r8c7вЂ”r8c9).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c6, [r8c5=r9c8], r9c4, r9c3} (spine [r8c5=r9c8]вЂ”r9c4).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c3, r5c6, [r7c6=r9c3]} (spine r5c3вЂ”r5c6).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color([r7c6=r9c3]). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c9=r7c6=r9c3], r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c8} (spine r7c9вЂ”r9c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r6c8, r9c5} = {r7c6, r7c9}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, [r5c9=r8c8]вЂ”r6c8}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Diamond {r8c5, r7c6, r9c5, r9c3} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c5).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c9=r8c8], [r8c5=r9c3]} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c9=r8c8]).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r8c5=r9c3]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r5c9=r8c8], r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], r6c3} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c9=r6c3=r8c8], r6c6} (spine [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]вЂ”[r5c9=r6c3=r8c8]).
      в†’ color(r5c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r5c9=r6c3=r8c8], [r5c3=r6c6], [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], r7c6} (spine [r5c3=r6c6]вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r6c3=r8c8]) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  9.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c9=r6c3=r7c6=r8c8], r9c5, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c8, r7c9:
      Case A: color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c9, [r6c8=r7c9], r6c3}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, [r6c8=r7c9]} and {r5c9, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color([r6c8=r7c9]) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]}: [r6c8=r7c9]=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              col 9 {r5c9, [r6c8=r7c9], r9c9}: [r6c8=r7c9]=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5, r9c5в‰ X
              box 9 {[r6c8=r7c9], r8c8, r9c9}: [r6c8=r7c9]=X в†’ r8c8в‰ X
              [r6c8=r7c9]=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r5c3, r5c6в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              col 9 {r5c9, [r6c8=r7c9], r9c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5, r9c5в‰ X
              box 9 {[r6c8=r7c9], r8c8, r9c9}: [r6c8=r7c9], r9c9в‰ X в†’ r8c8=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c8) в‰  color(r7c9). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r6c6}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X в†’ r5c3, r5c9в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              r6c8=X в†’ r7c9, r8c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c3=X в†’ r9c5, r9c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {r5c9, r7c9, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c6) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r5c3, r5c6в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6=X в†’ r6c3, r6c8в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r6c6=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r5c9, r7c9, r9c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r7c9, r9c9в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c9}: r7c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r8c8=X
              r8c8=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c3=X в†’ r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c8} (spine r7c9вЂ”r9c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, [r5c9=r8c8]вЂ”r6c8}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r5c6, r6c3вЂ”r6c6, r9c3вЂ”r7c6}:
      Satellites [r5c9=r8c8] (rung 1), r6c8 (rung 2), r9c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r9c5 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r5c9=r8c8], r8c5, r7c6, r9c3, r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c8} (spine r7c9вЂ”r9c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r6c8, r9c4} = {r7c6, r7c9}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, [r5c9=r8c8]вЂ”r6c8}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Diamond {r8c5, r7c6, r9c4, r9c3} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c4).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c9=r8c8], [r8c5=r9c3]} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c9=r8c8]).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r8c5=r9c3]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r5c9=r8c8], r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], r6c3} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c9=r6c3=r8c8], r6c6} (spine [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]вЂ”[r5c9=r6c3=r8c8]).
      в†’ color(r5c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r5c9=r6c3=r8c8], [r5c3=r6c6], [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], r7c6} (spine [r5c3=r6c6]вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r6c3=r8c8]) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  9.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c9=r6c3=r7c6=r8c8], r9c4, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Diamond {r8c5, r7c6, r9c5, r9c3} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c5).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c9=r8c7], [r8c5=r9c3]} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c9=r8c7]).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r8c5=r9c3]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r5c9=r8c7], r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], r6c3} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c9=r6c3=r8c7], r6c6} (spine [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]вЂ”[r5c9=r6c3=r8c7]).
      в†’ color(r5c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r5c9=r6c3=r8c7], [r5c3=r6c6], [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], r7c6} (spine [r5c3=r6c6]вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r6c3=r8c7]) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  9.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c9=r6c3=r7c6=r8c7], r9c5, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r7c6, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r7c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c6, [r7c6=r9c9], r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r6c3, [r7c6=r9c9]} and {r6c6, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c3) = color([r7c6=r9c9]) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c8в‰ X
              row 9 {[r7c6=r9c9], r9c3, r9c5}: [r7c6=r9c9]=X в†’ r9c3, r9c5в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r5c3в‰ X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, [r7c6=r9c9]}: [r7c6=r9c9]=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r5c9, [r7c6=r9c9], r7c9}: [r7c6=r9c9]=X в†’ r5c9, r7c9в‰ X
              box 8 {[r7c6=r9c9], r8c5, r9c5}: [r7c6=r9c9]=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X
              box 9 {[r7c6=r9c9], r7c9, r8c7}: [r7c6=r9c9]=X в†’ r8c7в‰ X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c6) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6=X в†’ r6c3, r6c8в‰ X
              row 9 {[r7c6=r9c9], r9c3, r9c5}: r9c3=X в†’ [r7c6=r9c9], r9c5в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r5c3в‰ X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, [r7c6=r9c9]}: r6c6=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
              box 8 {[r7c6=r9c9], r8c5, r9c5}: [r7c6=r9c9], r9c5в‰ X в†’ r8c5=X
              r8c5=X в†’ r8c7в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c6в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
              col 9 {r5c9, [r7c6=r9c9], r7c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
              box 9 {[r7c6=r9c9], r7c9, r8c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r7c6) в‰  color(r9c9). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c9, r6c8, r6c3}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5, r9c5в‰ X
              r7c6=X в†’ r7c9, r9c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X

              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c8в‰ X

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r7c9вЂ”r9c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, [r5c9=r8c7]вЂ”r6c8}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r5c6, r6c3вЂ”r6c6, r9c3вЂ”r7c6}:
      Satellites [r5c9=r8c7] (rung 1), r6c8 (rung 2), r9c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r9c5 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r5c9=r8c7], r8c5, r7c6, r9c3, r9c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c7} (spine r7c9вЂ”r9c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r6c8, r9c4} = {r7c6, r7c9}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, [r5c9=r8c7]вЂ”r6c8}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  4.  Diamond {r8c5, r7c6, r9c4, r9c3} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c4).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c9=r8c7], [r8c5=r9c3]} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c9=r8c7]).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r8c5=r9c3]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r5c9=r8c7], r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], r6c3} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r8c7]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c9=r6c3=r8c7], r6c6} (spine [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]вЂ”[r5c9=r6c3=r8c7]).
      в†’ color(r5c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r5c9=r6c3=r8c7], [r5c3=r6c6], [r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], r7c6} (spine [r5c3=r6c6]вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r6c3=r8c7]) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  9.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c9=r6c3=r7c6=r8c7], r9c4, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r5c3, r5c6в‰ X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c5, r8c8в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c2], r8c3}: r8c3=X в†’ [r6c3=r9c2]в‰ X
        r5c9=X в†’ [r6c8=r9c7], r7c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c6], [r6c8=r9c7]}: [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c8=r9c7]в‰ X в†’ [r6c5=r9c6]=X
        box 9 {[r6c8=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Parity chain deduction:
      row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}
      row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}
      Parallel links: row 5в†”row 6 (col, box, box); row 6в†”row 8 (col, col, col); row 8в†”row 9 (box, box, box)
      в†’ same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column в†’ distinct permutations.
      Only non-colliding pair: row 6 and row 9 в†’ same permutation.
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c9, [r6c8=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8} (spine [r6c8=r9c7]вЂ”r7c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c9=r8c8], r8c3} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c9=r8c8]).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c9=r8c8], r5c3, [r5c6=r8c3], [r6c3=r9c2]} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c6=r8c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r8c8]) = color([r6c3=r9c2]). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r8c3], [r5c9=r6c3=r8c8=r9c2], [r6c5=r9c6], r8c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+oddwheel+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Parity chain deduction:
      row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
      row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c8}
      row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}
      Parallel links: row 5в†”row 6 (col, box, box); row 6в†”row 8 (col, col, col); row 8в†”row 9 (box, box, box)
      в†’ same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column в†’ distinct permutations.
      Only non-colliding pair: row 6 and row 9 в†’ same permutation.
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c9, [r6c8=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8} (spine [r6c8=r9c7]вЂ”r7c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, [r5c9=r8c8], r8c3} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c9=r8c8]).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, [r5c9=r8c8], r8c5, [r6c5=r9c6], [r6c3=r9c2]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_parity] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c3, r8c5:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r8c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c9, r6c8, r8c8, r7c9}:
            Diagonals: {r5c9, r8c8} and {r6c8, r7c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c9) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r5c3, r5c6в‰ X
              row 8 {[r6c3=r8c5], r8c3, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ [r6c3=r8c5], r8c3в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r8c5], r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r7c9) = X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r8c5], r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ [r6c3=r8c5], r6c5в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9=X в†’ r8c8, r9c7в‰ X
              r6c8=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 8 {[r6c3=r8c5], r8c3, r8c8}: [r6c3=r8c5], r8c8в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r8c5], r8c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r5c3в‰ X
              r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
              r5c6=X and r9c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) в‰  color(r8c5). Add edge.
        3.  Diamond {r6c5, r6c3, r8c5, r8c3} (spine r6c3вЂ”r8c5).
            в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, [r6c5=r8c3], r8c5, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r8c5} and {[r6c5=r8c3], r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X в†’ r5c3, r5c9в‰ X
              row 8 {[r6c5=r8c3], r8c5, r8c8}: r8c5=X в†’ [r6c5=r8c3], r8c8в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, [r6c5=r8c3]}: r5c3, [r6c5=r8c3]в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              r5c6=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c3=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c3=X and r8c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r6c5=r8c3]) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r8c3], r6c8}: [r6c5=r8c3]=X в†’ r6c3, r6c8в‰ X
              row 8 {[r6c5=r8c3], r8c5, r8c8}: [r6c5=r8c3]=X в†’ r8c5, r8c8в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6=X в†’ r9c2, r9c7в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, [r6c5=r8c3]}: [r6c5=r8c3]=X в†’ r5c3в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r7c9=X
              [r6c5=r8c3]=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r7c9=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r9c3, r5c6вЂ”r9c6, r5c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Branch on r5c9, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r7c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, r5c6, r9c6, r9c9, r7c8, r6c8, r6c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) в‰  color(r7c6). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X в†’ r5c3, r5c9в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, r6c5в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r7c6, r9c6в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r8c5=X
              r6c8=X в†’ r7c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c5=X в†’ r8c7в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c3=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r5c3, r5c6в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c3, r6c8в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              r5c9=X в†’ r7c6, r9c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c5=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c3=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r9c3, [r5c6=r8c5]вЂ”r9c6, r5c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Branch on r5c9, r7c8:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], [r5c9=r7c8], r7c6} (spine [r5c6=r8c5]вЂ”[r5c9=r7c8]).
            в†’ color(r5c3) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {[r5c9=r7c8], [r5c3=r7c6], [r5c6=r8c5], r9c6} (spine [r5c3=r7c6]вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5]).
            в†’ color([r5c9=r7c8]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        6.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c5], [r5c3=r7c6], [r5c9=r7c8=r9c6], r9c3} (spine [r5c3=r7c6]вЂ”[r5c9=r7c8=r9c6]).
            в†’ color([r5c6=r8c5]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        7.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r8c5=r9c3], [r5c9=r7c8=r9c6], r8c7, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) в‰  color(r7c8). Add edge.
        8.  Diamond {r6c8, r5c9, r7c8, r9c9} (spine r5c9вЂ”r7c8).
            в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r6c5, r6c3, [r6c8=r9c9], r9c3} (spine r6c3вЂ”[r6c8=r9c9]).
            в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], [r6c5=r9c3]} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5]).
            в†’ color(r5c9) = color([r6c5=r9c3]). Identify.
        11.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c5], r5c3, [r5c9=r6c5=r9c3], r6c3} (spine r5c3вЂ”[r5c9=r6c5=r9c3]).
            в†’ color([r5c6=r8c5]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        12.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r6c3=r8c5], [r5c9=r6c5=r9c3], [r6c8=r9c9], r9c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r6c5:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c6, r7c8, r8c7, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {r7c6, r8c7} and {r7c8, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c7) = X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6=X в†’ r5c6, r9c6в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r8c7=X в†’ r7c8, r9c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r6c5], r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ [r5c3=r6c5], r6c3в‰ X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r6c5], r5c6, r5c9}: [r5c3=r6c5], r5c6в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
              row 6 {[r5c3=r6c5], r6c3, r6c8}: [r5c3=r6c5], r6c3в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
              r5c9=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c8) = color(r8c5) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r8c5=X в†’ r7c6, r9c6в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X в†’ r8c7, r9c9в‰ X
              r7c8=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c5=X в†’ [r5c3=r6c5]в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r5c3=r6c5], r6c3, r6c8}: [r5c3=r6c5], r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c9}: r9c6, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r6c5], r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) в‰  color(r6c5). Add edge.
        3.  Guardian: r9c6 guards oddagon {r9c6, r5c6, r6c5, r6c3, r9c3} (source r5c3).
            в†’ color(r9c6) = color(r5c3). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c3=r9c6], r7c6, r7c8, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c3=r9c6], r7c8} and {r7c6, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c3=r9c6]) = color(r7c8) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r9c6], r5c6, r5c9}: [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X
              row 9 {[r5c3=r9c6], r9c3, r9c9}: [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r9c3, r9c9в‰ X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r9c6], r6c3, r9c3}: [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 6 {[r5c3=r9c6], r5c6, r7c6}: [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              box 8 {[r5c3=r9c6], r7c6, r8c5}: [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X в†’ r8c7в‰ X
              [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r7c8=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c6) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {[r5c3=r9c6], r9c3, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ [r5c3=r9c6], r9c3в‰ X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r9c6], r6c3, r9c3}: [r5c3=r9c6], r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              col 6 {[r5c3=r9c6], r5c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
              box 8 {[r5c3=r9c6], r7c6, r8c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r7c8, r8c7в‰ X
              r6c3=X в†’ r6c5, r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c9=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 5 {[r5c3=r9c6], r5c6, r5c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  Branch on [r5c6=r8c5], r9c9:
      Case A: color([r5c6=r8c5]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c5=r9c9], r5c3, r9c3, r6c3} (spine r5c3вЂ”r9c3).
            в†’ color([r5c6=r8c5=r9c9]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r6c3=r8c5=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, r5c9, r6c8, r7c8, r7c6, r9c6, r9c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r5c6=r8c5]) в‰  color(r9c9). Add edge.
        5.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r5c9, [r5c6=r8c5], r8c7, r7c8} (source r9c9).
            в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        6.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r5c6=r8c5], r6c5, r6c3, r9c3, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c8, r7c8, r9c8, r8c7} (spine r7c8вЂ”r9c8).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r8c5], r5c9, [r6c8=r8c7]} (spine [r5c6=r8c5]вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r5c3) = color([r6c8=r8c7]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c3=r6c8=r8c7], [r5c6=r8c5], r6c5} (spine [r5c3=r6c8=r8c7]вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5]).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c5). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c5], [r5c3=r6c8=r8c7], [r5c9=r6c5], r6c3} (spine [r5c3=r6c8=r8c7]вЂ”[r5c9=r6c5]).
      в†’ color([r5c6=r8c5]) = color(r6c3). Identify.

      в†’ color([r5c9=r6c5]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c3=r8c5], [r5c3=r6c8=r8c7], [r5c9=r6c5=r9c3], r9c8} (spine [r5c3=r6c8=r8c7]вЂ”[r5c9=r6c5=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c3=r8c5]) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r5c9=r6c5=r9c3], [r5c3=r6c8=r8c7], [r5c6=r6c3=r8c5=r9c8], r7c8} (spine [r5c3=r6c8=r8c7]вЂ”[r5c6=r6c3=r8c5=r9c8]).
      в†’ color([r5c9=r6c5=r9c3]) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  9.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r6c3=r8c5=r9c8], [r5c9=r6c5=r7c8=r9c3], r7c6, r9c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c8) = color(r8c5) = X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r8c5=X в†’ r7c6, r9c6в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c7}: r7c8=X в†’ r8c7, r9c7в‰ X
              r7c8=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c5=X в†’ r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8, r7c8} (spine [r6c5=r7c6]вЂ”r6c8).
            в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c3, [r6c3=r7c8], r9c3, r9c7} (spine [r6c3=r7c8]вЂ”r9c3).
            в†’ color(r5c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c3=r9c7], [r5c6=r8c5], r8c7} (spine [r5c3=r9c7]вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5]).
            в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        6.  Diamond {[r5c9=r8c7], [r5c3=r9c7], [r5c6=r8c5], r9c6} (spine [r5c3=r9c7]вЂ”[r5c6=r8c5]).
            в†’ color([r5c9=r8c7]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        7.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c3=r9c7], [r5c9=r8c7=r9c6], [r6c3=r7c8], r9c3}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) в‰  color(r7c6). Add edge.
        8.  Diamond {r6c5, [r5c6=r8c5], r7c6, r9c6} (spine [r5c6=r8c5]вЂ”r7c6).
            в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r6c8, r6c3, [r6c5=r9c6], r9c3} (spine r6c3вЂ”[r6c5=r9c6]).
            в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        10.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c5], r5c3, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c3]} (spine r5c3вЂ”r5c9).
            в†’ color([r5c6=r8c5]) = color([r6c8=r9c3]). Identify.
        11.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r6c8=r8c5=r9c3], r7c8, r8c7, r9c7}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_guardian] (ladder+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        2.  Circular ladder {[r6c5=r7c6]вЂ”r7c8, r8c5вЂ”r8c7, r9c6вЂ”r9c7}:
            Satellites r6c8 (rung 1), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c8, r7c8, r9c7, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r6c8, r9c7} and {r7c8, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c8) = color(r9c7) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6]в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c7=X в†’ r9c3, r9c6в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r5c3=X
              col 6 {r5c6, [r6c5=r7c6], r9c6}: [r6c5=r7c6], r9c6в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
              box 8 {[r6c5=r7c6], r8c5, r9c6}: [r6c5=r7c6], r9c6в‰ X в†’ r8c5=X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c7}: r9c7=X в†’ r7c8, r8c7в‰ X
              r5c3=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              r5c3=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c8) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c3=X в†’ r9c6, r9c7в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r5c3, r6c3в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c7}: r7c8=X в†’ r8c7в‰ X
              r7c8=X в†’ [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) в‰  color(r7c6). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X в†’ r5c3, r5c9в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, r6c5в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r7c6, r9c6в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r8c5=X
              r6c8=X в†’ r7c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c5=X в†’ r8c7в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c3=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r5c3, r5c6в‰ X

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r6c8:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r9c6 guards oddagon {r9c6, r5c6, r6c5, r6c3, r9c3} (source [r5c3=r6c8]).
            в†’ color(r9c6) = color([r5c3=r6c8]). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c3=r6c8=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r6c5, r6c3, r9c3, r9c7, r7c8, r7c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) в‰  color(r6c8). Add edge.
        4.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r5c3вЂ”r5c9).
            в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        5.  Circular ladder {r7c6вЂ”r7c8, r8c5вЂ”r8c7, r9c6вЂ”r9c7}:
            Satellites [r5c6=r6c8] (rung 1), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        6.  Kв‚„ on {r5c3, [r5c6=r6c8], r6c3, r9c3}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
              r5c6=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r5c3=r6c5], r6c3, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) в‰  color(r6c5). Add edge.
        3.  Guardian: r9c6 guards oddagon {r9c6, r5c6, r6c5, r6c3, r9c3} (source r5c3).
            в†’ color(r9c6) = color(r5c3). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c3=r9c6], r7c6, r7c8, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c3=r9c6], r7c8} and {r7c6, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c3=r9c6]) = color(r7c8) = X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r9c6], r5c6, r5c9}: [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X
              row 9 {[r5c3=r9c6], r9c3, r9c9}: [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r9c3, r9c9в‰ X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r9c6], r6c3, r9c3}: [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 6 {[r5c3=r9c6], r5c6, r7c6}: [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              box 8 {[r5c3=r9c6], r7c6, r8c4}: [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r8c4в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r7c8=X в†’ r8c7в‰ X
              [r5c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r7c8=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c6) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {[r5c3=r9c6], r9c3, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ [r5c3=r9c6], r9c3в‰ X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r9c6], r6c3, r9c3}: [r5c3=r9c6], r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              col 6 {[r5c3=r9c6], r5c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
              box 8 {[r5c3=r9c6], r7c6, r8c4}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c4в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r7c8, r8c7в‰ X
              r6c3=X в†’ r6c5, r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c9=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 5 {[r5c3=r9c6], r5c6, r5c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Branch on [r5c6=r8c4], r9c9:
      Case A: color([r5c6=r8c4]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {[r5c6=r8c4=r9c9], r5c3, r9c3, r6c3} (spine r5c3вЂ”r9c3).
            в†’ color([r5c6=r8c4=r9c9]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r6c3=r8c4=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, r5c9, r6c8, r7c8, r7c6, r9c6, r9c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r5c6=r8c4]) в‰  color(r9c9). Add edge.
        5.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r5c9, [r5c6=r8c4], r8c7, r7c8} (source r9c9).
            в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        6.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r5c6=r8c4], r6c5, r6c3, r9c3, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.

      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r8c7). Identify.

      в†’ color(r5c3) = color([r6c8=r8c7]). Identify.

      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c5). Identify.

      в†’ color([r5c6=r8c4]) = color(r6c3). Identify.

      в†’ color([r5c9=r6c5]) = color(r9c3). Identify.

      в†’ color([r5c9=r6c5=r9c3]) = color(r7c8). Identify.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6, r8c4} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        3.  Diamond {r6c3, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c8, r7c8} (spine [r6c5=r7c6]вЂ”r6c8).
            в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c3, [r6c3=r7c8], r9c3, r9c7} (spine [r6c3=r7c8]вЂ”r9c3).
            в†’ color(r5c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c3=r9c7], [r5c6=r8c4], r8c7} (spine [r5c3=r9c7]вЂ”[r5c6=r8c4]).
            в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c7). Identify.
        6.  Diamond {[r5c9=r8c7], [r5c3=r9c7], [r5c6=r8c4], r9c6} (spine [r5c3=r9c7]вЂ”[r5c6=r8c4]).
            в†’ color([r5c9=r8c7]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        7.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c3=r9c7], [r5c9=r8c7=r9c6], [r6c3=r7c8], r9c3}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) в‰  color(r7c6). Add edge.
        8.  Diamond {r6c5, [r5c6=r8c4], r7c6, r9c6} (spine [r5c6=r8c4]вЂ”r7c6).
            в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        9.  Diamond {r6c8, r6c3, [r6c5=r9c6], r9c3} (spine r6c3вЂ”[r6c5=r9c6]).
            в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c3). Identify.

            в†’ color([r5c6=r8c4]) = color([r6c8=r9c3]). Identify.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_guardian] (ladder+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c9, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        2.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c5, [r5c9=r7c6]вЂ”r6c8}:
            Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r7c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c3, r6c8, r7c8, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r6c3, r7c8} and {r6c8, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c3) = color(r7c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c5, r6c8в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r5c3, r9c3в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c7}: r7c8=X в†’ r8c7, r9c7в‰ X
              r7c8=X в†’ [r5c9=r7c6]в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c9=r7c6]}: r5c3, [r5c9=r7c6]в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c3, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
              col 6 {r5c6, [r5c9=r7c6], r9c6}: r5c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, r6c5в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c3=X в†’ r9c6, r9c7в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r5c3в‰ X
              r6c8=X в†’ [r5c9=r7c6], r7c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, [r5c9=r7c6]}: r5c3, [r5c9=r7c6]в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
              box 8 {[r5c9=r7c6], r8c4, r9c6}: [r5c9=r7c6], r9c6в‰ X в†’ r8c4=X
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c7}: r7c8, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r8c7=X
              r8c4=X and r8c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) в‰  color(r7c6). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X в†’ r5c3, r5c9в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, r6c5в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3, r6c3в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 6 {r5c6, r7c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r7c6, r9c6в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c6}: r7c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r8c4=X
              r6c8=X в†’ r7c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c4=X в†’ r8c7в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c3=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 9 {r7c8, r8c7, r9c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===

            в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.

            Satellites [r5c6=r6c8] (rung 1), r9c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  1.  Branch on r7c6, r8c9:

            в†’ color(r5c3) = color([r7c6=r8c9]). Identify.
        3.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c3=r7c6=r8c9], r9c3, r9c5, r9c8}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r7c6) в‰  color(r8c9). Add edge.
        4.  Diamond {r8c9, r7c6, r8c4, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c4).
            в†’ color(r8c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {r7c7, r7c6, [r8c9=r9c5], r8c4} (spine r7c6вЂ”[r8c9=r9c5]).
            в†’ color(r7c7) = color(r8c4). Identify.

            в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        7.  Trivalue oddagon:
            row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}
              в†’ via boxes {5, 6} [even]
            row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}
              в†’ via cols {3, 5, 8} [odd]
            row 9 {[r7c6=r9c8], [r8c9=r9c5], r9c3}
              в†’ via cols {3} [even]
            Cycle parity: odd. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r7c9, r9c9, r8c8} (spine r7c9вЂ”r9c9).

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Branch on r5c9, r6c3:

            Satellites [r5c9=r6c3] (rung 1), r9c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.

            Bivalue oddagon {r5c3, r5c6, r6c5, r9c5, r9c9, r7c9, r7c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

            в†’ color([r5c9=r7c3]) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        6.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c9=r6c5=r7c3], r9c2, r9c5, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r8c3=r9c5], [r8c5=r9c3], r8c8, r9c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c5=r9c6]} = {[r6c8=r7c9]}.
      в†’ color([r6c5=r9c6]) = color([r6c8=r7c9]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
        r8c3=X в†’ r8c7, r8c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r8c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r6c6, r9c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, [r6c5=r9c6], r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]} (spine [r6c5=r9c6]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r7c9]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c8=r7c9], r8c7, r8c8, r8c3} (spine r8c7вЂ”r8c8).
      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r6c8=r7c9=r8c3], [r6c5=r9c6], r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r6c6, r9c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c5=r9c6]} = {[r6c8=r7c9]}.
      в†’ color([r6c5=r9c6]) = color([r6c8=r7c9]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r6c6, r8c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c5=r8c6]} = {[r6c8=r7c9]}.
      в†’ color([r6c5=r8c6]) = color([r6c8=r7c9]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 6 {r6c5, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c5, r6c6в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r8c6в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
        r6c8=X в†’ r9c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r4c9, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r7c9=X
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r7c9=X в†’ r8c7в‰ X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c6, r8c7в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
        row 6 {r6c5, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c6, r6c8в‰ X
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r4c9, r7c9в‰ X

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r6c6, r8c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, [r6c5=r8c6], r6c6, [r6c8=r7c9]} (spine [r6c5=r8c6]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r7c9]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c6, [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9], [r6c5=r8c6], r8c7} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]вЂ”[r6c5=r8c6]).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c5=r8c6], [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9], [r6c6=r8c7], r9c8} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]вЂ”[r6c6=r8c7]).
      в†’ color([r6c5=r8c6]) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c5=r8c6=r9c8], r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў
