        r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c4в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c5}: r7c6, r8c4в‰ X в†’ r9c5=X
        r9c5=X в†’ r9c2, r9c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
        row 6 {r6c5, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c6, r6c8в‰ X
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r4c9, r8c9в‰ X
        box 5 {r5c6, r6c5, r6c6}: r6c5=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
        r6c5=X в†’ r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r5c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c4в‰ X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c9}: r8c4, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2, r9c3в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c6, [r6c8=r8c9]} (spine [r6c5=r7c6]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r8c9]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c6, [r5c6=r6c8=r8c9], [r6c5=r7c6], r8c4} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r8c9]вЂ”[r6c5=r7c6]).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r8c4). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c5=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c8=r8c9], [r6c6=r8c4], r8c3} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r8c9]вЂ”[r6c6=r8c4]).
      в†’ color([r6c5=r7c6]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c5=r7c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3, r9c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {[r6c8=r8c9]} = {[r6c5=r7c6]}.
      в†’ color([r6c8=r8c9]) = color([r6c5=r7c6]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, [r6c5=r7c6], r6c6, [r6c8=r9c9]} (spine [r6c5=r7c6]вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r6c5=r7c6], r8c4, r8c5, r8c3} (spine r8c4вЂ”r8c5).
      в†’ color([r6c5=r7c6]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r6c8=r9c9], [r6c5=r7c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r9c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {[r6c8=r9c9]} = {[r6c5=r7c6]}.
      в†’ color([r6c8=r9c9]) = color([r6c5=r7c6]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r4c9, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
        r6c8=X в†’ r6c4, r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, [r6c8=r7c9]} (spine r6c4вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r7c9]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c8, [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9], r8c6, r9c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r8c8) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r8c6, [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9], [r8c8=r9c6], r9c7} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]вЂ”[r8c8=r9c6]).
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {r8c3, [r8c6=r9c7], [r8c8=r9c6], r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c2, r8c6вЂ”r9c6, r8c8вЂ”r9c7}:
      Satellites r5c6 (rung 2), [r6c8=r7c9] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Kв‚„ on {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, [r6c8=r7c9]}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r8c6}:

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, [r6c8=r7c9]} (spine r6c4вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r7c9]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c6, [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9], r8c7, r9c8} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]вЂ”r8c7).
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {r8c3, [r8c6=r9c8], r9c2, r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 6, row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 5, box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r6c8, r8c6} = {r5c6, r7c9}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r5c9, r6c8, r8c6}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c8} and {r5c9, r8c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 6 {r6c4, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c4, r6c5в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
        r5c6=X в†’ r7c9, r8c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        r6c8=X в†’ r9c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r7c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c7}: r8c6=X в†’ r8c3, r8c7в‰ X
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r4c9, r7c9в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r5c9=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c7, r9c8}: r7c9, r8c7в‰ X в†’ r9c8=X
        r5c9=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        r9c8=X в†’ r9c2, r9c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, [r6c8=r7c9]} (spine r6c4вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r7c9]). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {[r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]}.
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color([r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  2.  Diamond {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, [r6c8=r7c9]} (spine r6c4вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r7c9]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c6, [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9], r8c7, r9c7} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r7c9]вЂ”r8c7).
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {r8c3, [r8c6=r9c7], r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r7c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 6, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 5, box 7}.
      Remainder: {[r6c8=r7c9], r9c6} = {r5c6, r8c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c8=r7c9], r8c7, r8c6, r9c6}:
      Diagonals: {[r6c8=r7c9], r8c6} and {r8c7, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r6c8=r7c9]) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 6 {r6c4, r6c5, [r6c8=r7c9]}: [r6c8=r7c9]=X в†’ r6c4, r6c5в‰ X

  1.  Branch on r6c8, r8c7:

              col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X

        row 8 {[r6c8=r8c9], r8c3, r8c5}: r8c5=X в†’ [r6c8=r8c9], r8c3в‰ X
        box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: r5c6=X в†’ r6c4, r6c5в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5=X в†’ r7c6, r9c4в‰ X
        r5c6=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c4, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6) = X
        row 6 {r6c4, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c4, [r6c8=r8c9]в‰ X
        box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: r6c5=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5, r9c4в‰ X
        row 8 {[r6c8=r8c9], r8c3, r8c5}: [r6c8=r8c9], r8c5в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2, r9c3в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c4}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, [r6c8=r8c9]} (spine r6c4вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r8c9]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c4, [r5c6=r6c8=r8c9], r6c5, r8c5} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r8c9]вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color(r6c4) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c5, [r5c6=r6c8=r8c9], [r6c4=r8c5], r7c6} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r8c9]вЂ”[r6c4=r8c5]).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c5=r7c6], [r5c6=r6c8=r8c9], [r6c4=r8c5], r8c3} (spine [r5c6=r6c8=r8c9]вЂ”[r6c4=r8c5]).
      в†’ color([r6c5=r7c6]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c5=r7c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3, r9c4}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r8c9} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color(r8c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r6c5, r8c5, [r7c6=r8c9]}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, r8c5} and {r6c5, [r7c6=r8c9]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5) = X
        row 8 {[r7c6=r8c9], r8c3, r8c5}: r8c5=X в†’ [r7c6=r8c9], r8c3в‰ X
        box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: r5c6=X в†’ r6c4, r6c5в‰ X
        box 8 {[r7c6=r8c9], r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5=X в†’ r9c4в‰ X
        r5c6=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c4, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c4, r6c5в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, [r7c6=r8c9]}: r5c9, [r7c6=r8c9]в‰ X в†’ r4c9=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r4c9=X and r6c8=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c5) = color([r7c6=r8c9]) = X
        row 6 {r6c4, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c4, r6c8в‰ X
        row 8 {[r7c6=r8c9], r8c3, r8c5}: [r7c6=r8c9]=X в†’ r8c3, r8c5в‰ X
        col 9 {r4c9, r5c9, [r7c6=r8c9]}: [r7c6=r8c9]=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
        box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: r6c5=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c8=r8c9]) = color(r7c6), but they share a house. Contradiction.

      Remainder: {[r5c6=r6c8=r9c9]} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c8=r9c9]) = color(r7c6), but they share a house. Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+guardian) ===

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r8c9} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color(r8c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.

  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c9=r6c5], r6c3, r8c3, [r7c6=r8c9]}:

        row 8 {[r7c6=r8c9], r8c3, r8c6}: r8c3=X в†’ [r7c6=r8c9], r8c6в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, [r7c6=r8c9], r8c6}: [r7c6=r8c9], r8c6в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r6c5], [r7c6=r8c9]}: [r5c9=r6c5]=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
        box 8 {[r7c6=r8c9], r8c6, r9c4}: [r7c6=r8c9], r8c6в‰ X в†’ r9c4=X
        r5c6=X and [r5c9=r6c5]=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c3) = color([r7c6=r8c9]) = X
        row 6 {[r5c9=r6c5], r6c3, r6c8}: r6c3=X в†’ [r5c9=r6c5], r6c8в‰ X
        row 8 {[r7c6=r8c9], r8c3, r8c6}: [r7c6=r8c9]=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, [r7c6=r8c9], r8c6}: [r7c6=r8c9]=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
        col 9 {r4c9, [r5c9=r6c5], [r7c6=r8c9]}: [r7c6=r8c9]=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, [r5c9=r6c5], r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r5c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r5c6 guards oddagon {r5c6, r6c5, r6c3, r8c3, r8c6} (source [r6c8=r8c9]).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color([r6c8=r8c9]). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {[r5c6=r6c8=r8c9]} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c8=r8c9]) = color(r7c6), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of Cв‚† requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r8c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, [r5c6=r9c5], r9c8, r9c3} (spine [r5c6=r9c5]вЂ”r9c8).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r9c3], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r9c3]вЂ”r6c7).
      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r6c3], r6c7, r7c7, r8c9}:
      Diagonals: {[r4c9=r6c3], r7c7} and {r6c7, r8c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r4c9=r6c3]) = color(r7c7) = X
        col 3 {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r9c3], r8c3}: [r4c9=r6c3]=X в†’ [r5c8=r9c3], r8c3в‰ X
        box 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r9c3], r6c7}: [r4c9=r6c3]=X в†’ r6c7в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r7c7=X в†’ r8c9, r9c8в‰ X
        r7c7=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c3, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r8c6=X
        row 9 {[r5c6=r9c5], [r5c8=r9c3], r9c8}: [r5c8=r9c3], r9c8в‰ X в†’ [r5c6=r9c5]=X
        col 6 {[r5c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c6}: [r5c6=r9c5]=X and r8c6=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
        box 6 {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r9c3], r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ [r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r9c3]в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c7, r8c9, r9c8}: r8c9=X в†’ r7c7, r9c8в‰ X
        row 9 {[r5c6=r9c5], [r5c8=r9c3], r9c8}: [r5c8=r9c3], r9c8в‰ X в†’ [r5c6=r9c5]=X
        col 3 {[r4c9=r6c3], [r5c8=r9c3], r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, r6c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r9c8:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r9c8], r8c9, r8c3, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c9=r9c8], r8c3} and {r8c9, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c9=r9c8]) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c5, r8c9в‰ X
              row 9 {[r4c9=r9c8], r9c3, r9c5}: [r4c9=r9c8]=X в†’ r9c3, r9c5в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r9c8], r6c9, r8c9}: [r4c9=r9c8]=X в†’ r6c9в‰ X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r9c8], r5c8, r6c9}: [r4c9=r9c8]=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5, r9c5в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              r7c6=X в†’ r5c6, r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c5в‰ X
              row 9 {[r4c9=r9c8], r9c3, r9c5}: r9c3=X в†’ [r4c9=r9c8], r9c5в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r9c8], r6c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r6c9в‰ X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r9c8], r5c8, r6c9}: [r4c9=r9c8], r6c9в‰ X в†’ r5c8=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r8c5, r9c5в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              r5c8=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r7c6=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) в‰  color(r9c8). Add edge.
        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c9, r6c6, r7c6, r9c5, r9c8}
            Bridges: r8c9вЂ”r8c5 (edges r4c9вЂ”r6c9 в†” r7c6вЂ”r9c5), r6c3вЂ”r9c3 (edges r6c9вЂ”r6c6 в†” r9c5вЂ”r9c8), r5c6вЂ”r5c8 (edges r6c6вЂ”r7c6 в†” r9c8вЂ”r4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of Cв‚† requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, r6c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r5c8=r8c9]вЂ”r9c8, r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c5}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 2), r7c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r6c3вЂ”r7c6, r8c3вЂ”r8c5, r9c3вЂ”r9c5}:
      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), [r5c8=r8c9] (rung 2), r9c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r6c6 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, [r5c8=r8c9], r6c9, r6c3, r7c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

            Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c5) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c5в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c7}: r9c5=X в†’ r9c3, r9c7в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X

              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c9}: r4c9, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r5c8=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c5}: r9c5=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              r5c8=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c3=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, r6c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r5c8=r8c9]вЂ”r9c7, r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c5}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 2), r7c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r6c3вЂ”r7c6, r8c3вЂ”r8c5, r9c3вЂ”r9c5}:
      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), [r5c8=r8c9] (rung 2), r9c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r6c6 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, [r5c8=r8c9], r6c9, r6c3, r7c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, r6c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r5c8=r8c9]вЂ”r9c8, r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c4}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 2), r7c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Diamond {r6c9, r6c3, r6c6, r7c6} (spine r6c3вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c9) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r5c8=r8c9], r5c6, [r6c9=r7c6], r6c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”[r6c9=r7c6]).
      в†’ color([r5c8=r8c9]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r6c9=r7c6], [r5c8=r6c6=r8c9], r6c3, r8c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c6=r8c9]вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color([r6c9=r7c6]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c6=r8c9], [r6c9=r7c6=r8c3], r5c6} (spine [r5c8=r6c6=r8c9]вЂ”[r6c9=r7c6=r8c3]).
      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r4c9=r5c6], [r5c8=r6c6=r8c9], [r6c9=r7c6=r8c3], r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c6=r8c9]вЂ”[r6c9=r7c6=r8c3]).
      в†’ color([r4c9=r5c6]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  8.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c6=r8c9], [r4c9=r5c6=r6c3], [r6c9=r7c6=r8c3], r9c3} (spine [r4c9=r5c6=r6c3]вЂ”[r6c9=r7c6=r8c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c8=r6c6=r8c9]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  9.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c8=r6c6=r8c9=r9c3], [r6c9=r7c6=r8c3], r8c5, r9c4}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (hexagon+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r9c8:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r9c8], r8c9, r8c3, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c9=r9c8], r8c3} and {r8c9, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c9=r9c8]) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c5, r8c9в‰ X
              row 9 {[r4c9=r9c8], r9c3, r9c4}: [r4c9=r9c8]=X в†’ r9c3, r9c4в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r9c8], r6c9, r8c9}: [r4c9=r9c8]=X в†’ r6c9в‰ X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r9c8], r5c8, r6c9}: [r4c9=r9c8]=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5, r9c4в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              r7c6=X в†’ r5c6, r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c5в‰ X
              row 9 {[r4c9=r9c8], r9c3, r9c4}: r9c3=X в†’ [r4c9=r9c8], r9c4в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r9c8], r6c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r6c9в‰ X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r9c8], r5c8, r6c9}: [r4c9=r9c8], r6c9в‰ X в†’ r5c8=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5, r9c4в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              r5c8=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r7c6=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) в‰  color(r9c8). Add edge.
        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r4c9, r6c9, r6c6, r7c6, r9c4, r9c8}
            Bridges: r8c9вЂ”r8c5 (edges r4c9вЂ”r6c9 в†” r7c6вЂ”r9c4), r6c3вЂ”r9c3 (edges r6c9вЂ”r6c6 в†” r9c4вЂ”r9c8), r5c6вЂ”r5c8 (edges r6c6вЂ”r7c6 в†” r9c8вЂ”r4c9).
            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of Cв‚† requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r9c4=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              r5c8=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c3=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, r6c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r5c8=r8c9]вЂ”r9c7, r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c4}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 2), r7c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Circular ladder {r6c3вЂ”r7c6, r8c3вЂ”r8c5, r9c3вЂ”r9c4}:
      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), [r5c8=r8c9] (rung 2), r9c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub r6c6 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, [r5c8=r8c9], r6c9, r6c3, r7c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r6c3вЂ”r8c3, r6c6вЂ”r8c6, r6c9вЂ”r8c9}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r5c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r5c6, r8c6, r8c9}:
      Diagonals: {r4c9, r8c6} and {r5c6, r8c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c6=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X в†’ r5c6, r6c6в‰ X
        col 9 {r4c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r4c9=X в†’ r6c9в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c9}: r4c9=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
        r4c9=X в†’ r7c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: r6c6, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
        r9c2=X в†’ r9c7, r9c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 9 {r8c9, r9c7, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

        r5c6=X в†’ r7c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, r6c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c8=r8c9], r5c6, r8c6, r6c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color([r5c8=r8c9]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c9, [r5c8=r6c6=r8c9], r6c3, r8c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c6=r8c9]вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color(r6c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c6=r8c9], [r6c9=r8c3], r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c6=r8c9]вЂ”[r6c9=r8c3]).
      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r5c8=r6c6=r8c9], [r4c9=r6c3], [r6c9=r8c3], r7c3} (spine [r4c9=r6c3]вЂ”[r6c9=r8c3]).
      в†’ color([r5c8=r6c6=r8c9]) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c8=r6c6=r7c3=r8c9], r9c2, r9c7, r9c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_ladder] (diamond+set+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, r6c9, r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c6, r5c6, [r5c8=r8c9], r8c3} (source r8c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c6]} = {r7c3}.
      в†’ color([r6c3=r8c6]) = color(r7c3), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c5, r8c9, r9c9, r9c4}:
      Diagonals: {r8c5, r9c9} and {r8c9, r9c4} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r8c5) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c3, r9c4в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        col 9 {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
        r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r6c6, r7c6в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r5c8=X
        r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c4) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c5в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4=X в†’ r9c3, r9c9в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        col 9 {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r9c4=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
        r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r6c6, r7c6в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r5c8=X
        r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c4, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r6c3, r7c6} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, [r6c8=r7c6], r6c3} (spine r4c9вЂ”[r6c8=r7c6]).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {r5c6, [r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, [r6c8=r7c6]}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c4, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r6c8, r4c9, r6c3, r7c6} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r7c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, r5c6, r6c6, r6c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c4, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r6c7, r4c9, r6c3, r7c6} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color(r6c7) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, [r6c7=r7c6], r6c3} (spine r4c9вЂ”[r6c7=r7c6]).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {r5c6, [r5c8=r6c3], r6c6, [r6c7=r7c6]}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c4, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r6c7, r4c9, r6c3, r7c6} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color(r6c7) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r7c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, r5c6, r6c6, r6c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        row 8 {[r7c3=r8c6], r8c3, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ [r7c3=r8c6], r8c3в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c7, r9c8}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c7, r9c8в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, [r7c3=r8c6], r8c3}: [r7c3=r8c6], r8c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c7в‰ X (adjacent)
        r8c9=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, [r7c3=r8c6]}: r6c6, [r7c3=r8c6]в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r6c7в‰ X в†’ r5c8=X
        r5c6=X and r5c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c7, r9c7} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]вЂ”r6c7).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c7, [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c7], r9c8} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]вЂ”[r6c6=r9c7]).
      в†’ color(r6c7) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, [r6c7=r9c8], r8c9} (spine r4c9вЂ”[r6c7=r9c8]).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {r5c6, [r5c8=r8c9], [r6c6=r9c7], r8c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_set] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c7, r9c7} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]вЂ”r6c7).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r5c8, r9c8, r8c9}:
      Diagonals: {r4c9, r9c8} and {r5c8, r8c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c7], r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c7]в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9=X в†’ r5c8, r6c7в‰ X
        box 9 {[r6c6=r9c7], r8c9, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c7], r6c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r5c8=X в†’ r4c9, r6c7в‰ X
        box 9 {[r6c6=r9c7], r8c9, r9c8}: r8c9=X в†’ [r6c6=r9c7], r9c8в‰ X
        r5c8=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], [r6c6=r9c7], r6c7}: [r6c6=r9c7], r6c7в‰ X в†’ [r6c3=r9c2]=X
        col 3 {[r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: [r6c3=r9c2]=X в†’ r7c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, [r6c6=r9c7], r8c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c3}.
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c7, r6c3, r6c6, [r7c3=r8c6]} (spine r6c3вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c7) = color([r7c3=r8c6]). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r7c3=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, r5c6, r6c6, r6c3, r8c3, r8c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+guardian) ===

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c4, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Guardian: r5c6 guards oddagon {r5c6, r5c8, r6c8, r6c3, r7c6} (source r4c9).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r4c9). Identify.
  3.  Kв‚„ on {[r4c9=r5c6], r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r6c5:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r6c5], r5c6, r7c6, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {[r4c9=r6c5], r7c6} and {r5c6, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c9=r6c5]) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 6 {[r4c9=r6c5], r6c3, r6c8}: [r4c9=r6c5]=X в†’ r6c3, r6c8в‰ X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r6c5], r8c9, r9c9}: [r4c9=r6c5]=X в†’ r8c9, r9c9в‰ X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r6c5], r5c8, r6c8}: [r4c9=r6c5]=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5, r9c4в‰ X
              [r4c9=r6c5]=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5=X в†’ r7c6, r9c4в‰ X
              r5c6=X в†’ [r4c9=r6c5], r5c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {[r4c9=r6c5], r8c9, r9c9}: [r4c9=r6c5], r8c9в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r6c5], r5c8, r6c8}: [r4c9=r6c5], r5c8в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
              r6c8=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c9=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) в‰  color(r6c5). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c5, r9c4, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c4} and {r8c5, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c4) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c5, r8c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4=X в†’ r9c3, r9c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r4c9=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r9c4=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              r4c9=X в†’ r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c5) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c3=X в†’ r9c4, r9c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X

              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c4, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, r6c8, r6c3} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r6c5, r8c5, r7c6}:
      Diagonals: {r5c6, r8c5} and {r6c5, r7c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5=X в†’ r7c6, r9c4в‰ X
        r5c6=X в†’ [r5c8=r6c3], r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r5c8=r6c3], r6c5, r6c8}: [r5c8=r6c3], r6c5в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
        col 3 {[r5c8=r6c3], r8c3, r9c3}: [r5c8=r6c3], r8c3в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
        box 6 {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c3], r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        r9c3=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 9 {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r7c6) = X
        row 6 {[r5c8=r6c3], r6c5, r6c8}: r6c5=X в†’ [r5c8=r6c3], r6c8в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c3], r6c8}: [r5c8=r6c3], r6c8в‰ X в†’ r4c9=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5, r9c4в‰ X
        r4c9=X в†’ r8c9, r9c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        r6c5=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        r4c9=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c6вЂ”r9c5, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r6c3, r8c3, r8c9}:
      Diagonals: {r4c9, r8c3} and {r6c3, r8c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c6, r8c9в‰ X

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r7c6, r8c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 6, row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r5c6=r9c5], r6c5, r6c7} = {r4c9, [r5c6=r9c5], r7c6}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Kв‚„ on {r4c9, [r5c6=r9c5], r5c8, r6c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r6c5:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r6c5], r5c6, r7c6, r8c5}:

            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r4c9=r6c5]) = color(r7c6) = X
              row 6 {[r4c9=r6c5], r6c3, r6c7}: [r4c9=r6c5]=X в†’ r6c3, r6c7в‰ X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r6c5], r8c9, r9c9}: [r4c9=r6c5]=X в†’ r8c9, r9c9в‰ X

              [r4c9=r6c5]=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r9c4}: r8c5=X в†’ r7c6, r9c4в‰ X
              r5c6=X в†’ [r4c9=r6c5], r5c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {[r4c9=r6c5], r8c9, r9c9}: [r4c9=r6c5], r8c9в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X
              box 6 {[r4c9=r6c5], r5c8, r6c7}: [r4c9=r6c5], r5c8в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
              r6c7=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c9=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c5, r9c4, r9c3}:

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c4, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:

              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c9}: r8c4=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c3=X в†’ r9c4, r9c9в‰ X

              col 9 {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r4c9=X
              box 6 {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c5], r6c7}: r4c9=X в†’ [r5c8=r6c5], r6c7в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r8c4=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              row 6 {[r5c8=r6c5], r6c3, r6c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c8) в‰  color(r6c5). Add edge.
        3.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c8, r6c5, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r6c5).
            в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c9, r9c9, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c9} and {r8c9, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c9}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c4, r8c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c3, r9c4в‰ X

              col 9 {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X

              r7c6=X в†’ [r5c6=r6c7]в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r5c6=r6c7], r6c3, r6c5}: [r5c6=r6c7], r6c3в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X

              r5c8=X and r6c5=X are adjacent

              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c4в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c3=X в†’ r9c4, r9c9в‰ X

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c7, r9c7} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]вЂ”r6c7).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r6c5=r9c7], r5c6, r9c6, r8c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color([r6c5=r9c7]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c7, [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r8c6=r9c7], r8c3} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]вЂ”[r6c5=r8c6=r9c7]).
      в†’ color(r6c7) = color(r8c3). Identify.

      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r5c8=r8c9], r5c6, [r6c5=r8c6=r9c7], r9c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”[r6c5=r8c6=r9c7]).
      в†’ color([r5c8=r8c9]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  7.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c8=r8c9=r9c6], [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c5=r8c6=r9c7], [r6c7=r8c3]}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

              box 9 {r7c9, r8c9, r9c7}: r9c7=X в†’ r7c9, r8c9в‰ X
              r5c8=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r8c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r6c3, r6c5в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c6, r9c7в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r4c9, r5c8в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c5, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c7, r9c7} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]вЂ”r6c7).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r4c9, r7c9, r8c9, [r6c5=r9c7]} (spine r7c9вЂ”r8c9).
      в†’ color(r4c9) = color([r6c5=r9c7]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r4c9=r6c5=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r5c8, r6c7, [r6c3=r9c2], r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.

  8.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c8=r6c6=r7c3=r8c9], r9c2, r9c7, r9c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_guardian] (set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r5c8, r8c3} = {r4c9, r6c6}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r6c6, r8c6, r8c9}:
      Diagonals: {r4c9, r8c6} and {r6c6, r8c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c6=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X

        Contradiction.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c7, r5c8, r5c6} (spine r5c7вЂ”r5c8).
      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c6, [r4c9=r5c6], r8c9, r8c3} (source r8c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {[r6c3=r8c6]} = {r7c3}.
      в†’ color([r6c3=r8c6]) = color(r7c3), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c7, r5c8, r5c6} (spine r5c7вЂ”r5c8).
      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c6, [r4c9=r5c6], r8c6, r8c9} (spine [r4c9=r5c6]вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r6c6=r8c9], r6c3, r8c3, r7c3} (spine r6c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color([r6c6=r8c9]) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c6=r7c3=r8c9], r9c2, r9c7, r9c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c3}.
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r5c6, [r7c3=r8c6], r8c9} = {r4c9, [r7c3=r8c6], r9c2}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r7c3=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r6c6, r6c3, r8c3, r8c9} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_set] (diamond+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c9, r9c7, r9c8, r9c2} (spine r9c7вЂ”r9c8).
      в†’ color(r8c9) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r6c6 guards oddagon {r6c6, r6c3, r7c3, [r8c9=r9c2], r8c6} (source r8c3).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r5c7, r9c7, [r8c9=r9c2]}:
      Diagonals: {r4c9, r9c7} and {r5c7, [r8c9=r9c2]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r9c7) = X
        row 9 {[r8c9=r9c2], r9c7, r9c8}: r9c7=X в†’ [r8c9=r9c2], r9c8в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c7, r5c8}: r4c9=X в†’ r5c7, r5c8в‰ X
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8}: r5c7, r5c8в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        col 6 {r5c6, [r6c6=r8c3], r8c6}: r5c6=X в†’ [r6c6=r8c3], r8c6в‰ X
        box 7 {[r6c6=r8c3], r7c3, [r8c9=r9c2]}: [r6c6=r8c3], [r8c9=r9c2]в‰ X в†’ r7c3=X
        r7c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 8 {[r6c6=r8c3], r8c6, [r8c9=r9c2]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c7) = color([r8c9=r9c2]) = X
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8}: r5c7=X в†’ r5c6, r5c8в‰ X
        row 8 {[r6c6=r8c3], r8c6, [r8c9=r9c2]}: [r8c9=r9c2]=X в†’ [r6c6=r8c3], r8c6в‰ X
        row 9 {[r8c9=r9c2], r9c7, r9c8}: [r8c9=r9c2]=X в†’ r9c7, r9c8в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, [r6c6=r8c3], r8c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  5.  Diamond {r6c5, [r4c9=r5c6], [r6c3=r8c6=r9c9], [r8c9=r9c3]} (spine [r4c9=r5c6]вЂ”[r6c3=r8c6=r9c9]).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color([r8c9=r9c3]). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c3=r8c6=r9c9], [r6c5=r8c9=r9c3], r7c5, r9c4}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder) ===

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c4, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:

      Case 1: color(r4c9) = color(r8c3) = X

            Diagonals: {[r5c6=r7c3], r9c5} and {r6c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c6=r7c3]) = color(r9c5) = X
              row 5 {[r5c6=r7c3], r5c7, r5c8}: [r5c6=r7c3]=X в†’ r5c7, r5c8в‰ X

              box 9 {r7c9, r8c9, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c5, r9c8в‰ X
              box 7 {[r5c6=r7c3], r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ [r5c6=r7c3], r8c3в‰ X
              r6c5=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 3 {[r5c6=r7c3], r6c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) в‰  color(r7c3). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c6, r6c5, r6c3, r7c3}:
            Diagonals: {r5c6, r6c3} and {r6c5, r7c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c6) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 5 {r5c6, r5c7, r5c8}: r5c6=X в†’ r5c7, r5c8в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X

              r9c2=X в†’ r9c5, r9c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c9, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r7c3) = X
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X в†’ r6c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              r6c5=X в†’ r5c6, r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r7c3=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c2, r9c5в‰ X в†’ r9c8=X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c9, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X

              box 6 {r4c9, r5c7, r5c8}: r4c9=X and r5c7=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r7c3=r8c9], [r7c9=r8c3], r9c2, r9c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c6} (spine r5c5вЂ”r5c6).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c5, r5c6, [r5c8=r6c6], r7c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”[r5c8=r6c6]).
      в†’ color(r5c5) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c4вЂ”r9c4, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), [r5c5=r7c6] (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {r4c9, [r5c5=r7c6], [r5c8=r6c6], r6c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c4вЂ”r9c4, r8c9вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r7c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Guardian: r4c9 guards oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, r5c5, r6c6, r7c6} (source r5c6).
      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  3.  Kв‚„ on {[r4c9=r5c6], r6c3, r6c6, r7c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r4c9, r5c6:
      Case A: color(r4c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c4, r8c9, r9c9, r9c4}:
            Diagonals: {r8c4, r9c9} and {r8c9, r9c4} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c4) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c9}: r8c4=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c3, r9c4в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r5c6], r8c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ [r4c9=r5c6]в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r8c4=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              r6c3=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {[r4c9=r5c6], r6c6, r7c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c4) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c4в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4=X в†’ r9c3, r9c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r5c6], r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9=X в†’ [r4c9=r5c6]в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r9c4=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              r6c3=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {[r4c9=r5c6], r6c6, r7c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) в‰  color(r5c6). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c4, r9c4, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c4} and {r8c4, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c4) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c9}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c4, r8c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c9}: r9c4=X в†’ r9c3, r9c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r4c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r8c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r4c9=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r9c4}: r9c4=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              r4c9=X в†’ r5c6, r5c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c6, r5c8в‰ X в†’ r5c5=X

              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c4) = color(r9c3) = X

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r9c2) = X
        col 9 {r4c9, r7c9, r8c9}: r7c9=X в†’ r4c9, r8c9в‰ X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c9, r9c8}: r7c9=X в†’ r9c8в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c7в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9, r6c7в‰ X в†’ r5c8=X
        r5c8=X в†’ r5c5, r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c6} (spine r5c5вЂ”r5c6).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r4c9, [r5c8=r6c6], r6c7, r6c3} (spine [r5c8=r6c6]вЂ”r6c7).
      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c9, [r4c9=r6c3], r7c3, r8c3} (spine [r4c9=r6c3]вЂ”r7c3).
      в†’ color(r7c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r7c3, [r4c9=r6c3], [r7c9=r8c3], r8c9} (spine [r4c9=r6c3]вЂ”[r7c9=r8c3]).
      в†’ color(r7c3) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r7c3=r8c9], [r7c9=r8c3], r9c2, r9c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.

      в†’ color([r6c3=r9c2]) = color([r4c9=r9c8]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c5, r5c6, r6c4} (spine r5c5вЂ”r5c6).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r6c4). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r4c9, r8c3:

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r4c9=r8c3], [r5c8=r6c4], r7c4, r8c6}:

              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r5c8=r6c4]) = color(r8c6) = X
              row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, [r5c8=r6c4]}: [r5c8=r6c4]=X в†’ r5c5, r5c6в‰ X
              row 8 {[r4c9=r8c3], r8c6, r8c9}: r8c6=X в†’ [r4c9=r8c3], r8c9в‰ X
              col 9 {[r4c9=r8c3], r8c9, r9c9}: [r4c9=r8c3], r8c9в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X

              r9c9=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 3 {[r4c9=r8c3], r6c3, r9c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r4c9) в‰  color(r8c3). Add edge.

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Circular ladder {r4c9вЂ”r9c3, r5c8вЂ”r5c3, r6c7вЂ”r6c3}:
      Satellites r9c9 (rung 1), r5c6 (rung 2), r6c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Branch on r5c6, r7c7:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r7c7). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r7c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c8, r6c7, r6c5, r9c9, r8c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) в‰  color(r7c7). Add edge.
        5.  Diamond {r7c6, r5c6, r7c7, r9c9} (spine r5c6вЂ”r7c7).
            в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.

            Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r6c5, r8c5, r8c8, r7c7} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c5, r5c8вЂ”r6c7}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Circular ladder {r4c9вЂ”r9c3, r5c8вЂ”r5c3, r6c7вЂ”r6c3}:
      Satellites r9c9 (rung 1), r5c6 (rung 2), r6c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Branch on r5c6, r7c7:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r7c7). Identify.
        4.  Diamond {r5c3, [r5c6=r7c7], r5c8, r6c7} (spine [r5c6=r7c7]вЂ”r5c8).
            в†’ color(r5c3) = color(r6c7). Identify.
        5.  Diamond {r5c8, r4c9, [r5c3=r6c7], r9c3} (spine r4c9вЂ”[r5c3=r6c7]).
            в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        6.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r7c7], [r5c8=r9c3], r8c8, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) в‰  color(r7c7). Add edge.
        7.  Diamond {r7c6, r5c6, r7c7, r9c9} (spine r5c6вЂ”r7c7).
            в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        8.  Diamond {r6c5, r5c6, [r7c6=r9c9], r7c7} (spine r5c6вЂ”[r7c6=r9c9]).
            в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r7c7). Identify.
        9.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c5=r7c7], [r7c6=r9c9], r8c5, r8c8}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (ladder+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c5, r5c8вЂ”r6c7}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Circular ladder {r4c9вЂ”r9c3, r5c8вЂ”r5c3, r6c7вЂ”r6c3}:
      Satellites r9c9 (rung 1), r5c6 (rung 2), r6c5 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Branch on r5c6, r7c7:
      Case A: color(r5c6) = color(r7c7). Identify.
        4.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r7c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r5c8, r6c7, r6c5, r9c9, r8c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c6) в‰  color(r7c7). Add edge.
        5.  Guardian: r8c5 guards oddagon {r8c5, r6c5, r9c9, r7c7, r7c6} (source r5c6).
            в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r5c6). Identify.
        6.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r8c5], r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c3, r8c8:
      Case A: color(r5c3) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        2.  Diamond {r7c7, [r5c3=r8c8], r9c9, r9c3} (spine [r5c3=r8c8]вЂ”r9c9).
            в†’ color(r7c7) = color(r9c3). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r4c9, r6c7, [r7c7=r9c3], r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {r4c9, [r7c7=r9c3]} and {r6c7, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r4c9) = color([r7c7=r9c3]) = X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r8c8], r6c3, [r7c7=r9c3]}: [r7c7=r9c3]=X в†’ [r5c3=r8c8], r6c3в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r4c9=X в†’ r5c8, r6c7в‰ X
              box 9 {[r5c3=r8c8], [r7c7=r9c3], r9c9}: [r7c7=r9c3]=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X
              [r7c7=r9c3]=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 5 {[r5c3=r8c8], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r8c8], r5c8в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c3, r6c7в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              r6c5=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r6c3, r6c5в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r4c9, r5c8в‰ X
              box 9 {[r5c3=r8c8], [r7c7=r9c3], r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ [r5c3=r8c8], [r7c7=r9c3]в‰ X
              row 5 {[r5c3=r8c8], r5c6, r5c8}: [r5c3=r8c8], r5c8в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
              col 3 {[r5c3=r8c8], r6c3, [r7c7=r9c3]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) в‰  color(r8c8). Add edge.
        4.  Diamond {r5c6, r5c3, r5c8, r8c8} (spine r5c3вЂ”r5c8).
            в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        5.  Guardian: r6c7 guards oddagon {r6c7, r6c5, r8c5, r7c6, r7c7} (source [r5c6=r8c8]).
            в†’ color(r6c7) = color([r5c6=r8c8]). Identify.
        6.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c6=r6c7=r8c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r4c9, r5c8, r5c3, r6c3, r6c5, r8c5, r7c6, r7c7, r9c9} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_oddwheel] (ladder+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c5, r5c8вЂ”r6c7}:

            в†’ color(r6c7) = color([r5c3=r8c8]). Identify.
        4.  Kв‚„ on {r4c9, [r5c3=r6c7=r8c8], r9c3, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c3) в‰  color(r8c8). Add edge.

            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c6) = color(r8c8) = X
              box 9 {r7c7, r8c8, r9c9}: r8c8=X в†’ r7c7, r9c9в‰ X

              Contradiction.

              r8c5=X в†’ r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c6, r8c6, r9c6, r9c5} (spine r8c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r3c8вЂ”r3c9, r5c8вЂ”r5c9, r8c8вЂ”r8c9}:
      Satellites r6c7 (rung 2), r8c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c6=r9c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c7, r8c6, r9c6, r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r5c9) = X
        col 8 {r3c8, r5c8, r8c8}: r3c8=X в†’ r5c8, r8c8в‰ X

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c6, r9c4, r9c5, r9c3} (spine r9c4вЂ”r9c5).
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c7, r6c3, r6c6, [r8c6=r9c3]} (spine r6c3вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c7) = color([r8c6=r9c3]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, r5c8, [r6c7=r8c6=r9c3], r8c8} (spine r5c8вЂ”[r6c7=r8c6=r9c3]).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c8, [r5c9=r8c8], r5c8} (spine r3c8вЂ”[r5c9=r8c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r5c8], [r5c9=r8c8], [r6c7=r8c6=r9c3], r8c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 6, row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 8}.
      Remainder: {r6c7, r8c9} = {r3c8, r5c8}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Diamond {r5c8, r5c9, r6c7, r8c9} (spine r5c9вЂ”r6c7).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c8, [r5c8=r8c9], r9c8} (spine r3c8вЂ”[r5c8=r8c9]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c8, [r3c9=r9c8], [r5c8=r8c9], r5c9} (spine [r3c9=r9c8]вЂ”[r5c8=r8c9]).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c8], [r3c8=r5c9], [r5c8=r8c9], r6c7} (spine [r3c8=r5c9]вЂ”[r5c8=r8c9]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r9c8]) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r6c6, [r3c9=r6c7=r9c8], r6c3, r9c3} (spine [r3c9=r6c7=r9c8]вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {r6c3, [r3c9=r6c7=r9c8], [r6c6=r9c3], r9c6} (spine [r3c9=r6c7=r9c8]вЂ”[r6c6=r9c3]).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  8.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c3=r9c6], [r6c6=r9c3], r8c3, r8c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: r5c6=X в†’ r6c4, r6c5в‰ X
        r5c6=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c5}: r6c4, r6c5в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
        r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r8c8) = X
        col 8 {r3c8, r5c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r3c8, r5c8в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r5c9, r7c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X
        row 5 {r5c6, r5c8, r5c9}: r5c8, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: r5c6=X в†’ r6c4, r6c5в‰ X
        r5c6=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c5}: r6c4, r6c5в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
        r6c3=X and r9c3=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, r6c3} (spine r6c4вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c3], r9c3, r9c6, r9c7} (spine r9c3вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c3]) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, [r5c6=r6c3=r9c7], r5c8, r8c8} (spine [r5c6=r6c3=r9c7]вЂ”r5c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c8, [r5c9=r8c8], r5c8} (spine r3c8вЂ”[r5c9=r8c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r5c8], [r5c6=r6c3=r9c7], [r5c9=r8c8], r7c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, r6c3} (spine r6c4вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r3c8вЂ”r3c9, r5c8вЂ”r5c9, r8c8вЂ”r7c9}:
      Satellites [r5c6=r6c3] (rung 2), r9c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r6c3], r9c3, r9c6, r9c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c8, r7c8, r8c8, r9c7} (spine r7c8вЂ”r8c8).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r3c8=r9c7]} = {r3c9}.
      в†’ color([r3c8=r9c7]) = color(r3c9), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r3c8, r8c8в‰ X

        r9c7=X в†’ r9c3, r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r5c3=X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r6c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
        r5c3=X and r5c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 5, row 6, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 8}.
      Remainder: {r5c9, r9c7} = {r3c8, r8c8}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9, r9c7} (spine r5c9вЂ”r7c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c7], r3c8, r8c8, r6c8} (spine r3c8вЂ”r8c8).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r9c7]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c6, [r3c9=r6c8=r9c7], r6c3, r9c3} (spine [r3c9=r6c8=r9c7]вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r6c3, [r3c9=r6c8=r9c7], [r6c6=r9c3], r9c6} (spine [r3c9=r6c8=r9c7]вЂ”[r6c6=r9c3]).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {r5c3, r5c6, [r6c3=r9c6], [r6c6=r9c3]}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder) ===

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r6c3=r9c2]вЂ”r9c7, r6c8вЂ”r8c8, r6c9вЂ”r7c9}:
      Satellites r9c6 (rung 1), r3c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, r9c6, r8c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Trivalue oddagon:
      row 6 {[r6c3=r9c2], r6c8, r6c9}
        в†’ via cols {8, 9} [odd]
      box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}
        в†’ via rows {7, 8} [even]
      col 3 {[r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}
        в†’ via rows {6, 7} [even]
      col 9 {[r3c9=r8c6], r6c9, r7c9}
        в†’ via boxes {9} [odd]
      row 9 {[r6c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c7}
        в†’ via boxes {7, 9} [odd]
      row 8 {[r3c9=r8c6], r8c3, r8c8}
        в†’ via cols {3, 8} [even]
      Cycle parity: odd. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c2, r8c6вЂ”r9c6, r8c8вЂ”r9c7}:

  2.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c9, r3c9, r3c6, r7c3} (source r7c9).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r7c9). Identify.

      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, r6c8, r8c8, r8c3, r7c3} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r8c3:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r8c3], r6c3, r6c8, r8c8}:

              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r9c7=X

              r9c2=X and r9c6=X are adjacent

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c8, r9c7, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c7} and {r8c8, r9c2} (non-adjacent).

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r3c6 (rung 2), r6c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r7c9, r7c3}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, r7c9} and {r3c9, r7c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r7c9) = X
        col 6 {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r8c6, r9c6в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X в†’ r3c9, r6c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c8}: r7c9=X в†’ r8c8, r9c8в‰ X
        r3c6=X в†’ r6c8, r7c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        row 8 {r8c2, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c6, r8c8в‰ X в†’ r8c2=X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6, r9c8в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
        col 8 {r6c8, r8c8, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r7c3) = X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r6c9, r7c9в‰ X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: r7c3=X в†’ r8c2, r9c2в‰ X
        r3c9=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        r7c3=X в†’ r6c3, r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r8c8, r9c8, r7c9} (spine r8c8вЂ”r9c8).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c9, r6c3, [r6c8=r7c9], r7c3} (spine r6c3вЂ”[r6c8=r7c9]).
      в†’ color(r6c9) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, [r6c8=r7c9], [r6c9=r7c3], r6c3} (spine [r6c8=r7c9]вЂ”[r6c9=r7c3]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Circular ladder {[r6c8=r7c9]вЂ”[r6c9=r7c3], r8c8вЂ”r8c2, r9c8вЂ”r9c2}:
      Satellites [r3c9=r6c3] (rung 1), r8c6 (rung 2), r9c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c3], r8c6, r9c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r8c8, r9c8, r7c9} (spine r8c8вЂ”r9c8).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c9, r6c3, [r6c8=r7c9], r7c3} (spine r6c3вЂ”[r6c8=r7c9]).
      в†’ color(r6c9) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c2, r8c8, r9c8, r9c2}:
      Diagonals: {r8c2, r9c8} and {r8c8, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r8c2) = color(r9c8) = X

        r3c6=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {r6c3, [r6c8=r7c9], [r6c9=r7c3]}: [r6c8=r7c9], [r6c9=r7c3]в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c8=r7c9], [r6c9=r7c3]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 8 {r8c2, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c2, r8c6в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c6, r9c8в‰ X

        col 8 {[r6c8=r7c9], r8c8, r9c8}: r8c8=X в†’ [r6c8=r7c9]в‰ X

        r3c6=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)

        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c8=r7c9], [r6c9=r7c3]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r8c8, r9c8, r7c9} (spine r8c8вЂ”r9c8).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r8c2вЂ”r9c2, r8c6вЂ”r9c6, r8c8вЂ”r9c8}:
      Satellites r7c3 (rung 1), r3c6 (rung 2), [r6c8=r7c9] (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r7c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, r6c3, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c5, r8c8вЂ”r9c8}:

  3.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r6c9=r7c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r6c9=r7c6]).

  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r6c6], r6c3, [r6c9=r7c6], r7c9}. Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c5вЂ”r9c5, r8c8вЂ”r9c8}:

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r8c3:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r9c4 guards oddagon {r9c4, r7c6, r6c6, r6c3, r9c3} (source [r3c6=r8c3]).
            в†’ color(r9c4) = color([r3c6=r8c3]). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r8c3=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c6, r7c6, r9c5, r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r8c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r7c9, r7c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r7c9} and {r3c9, r7c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r7c9) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r6c6, r7c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r7c9, r8c9}: r7c9=X в†’ r3c9, r8c9в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r8c9}: r7c9=X в†’ r8c8в‰ X
              r3c6=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c8, r8c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r7c6) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X в†’ r3c6, r6c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r7c9, r8c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r7c9, r8c9в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r9c4, r9c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r9c4, r9c5в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r8c9}: r7c9, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r8c8=X
              r8c8=X в†’ r6c8, r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c5}: r9c4, r9c5в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X and r9c3=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў
