=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r4c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r4c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c6, r8c8, r9c8, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {r8c6, r9c8} and {r8c8, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c6) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c6=X в†’ r8c3, r8c8в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r9c3, r9c6в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              col 6 {[r3c6=r4c9], r8c6, r9c6}: r8c6=X в†’ [r3c6=r4c9]в‰ X
              col 8 {r5c8, r8c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              box 6 {[r3c6=r4c9], r5c8, r6c7}: [r3c6=r4c9], r5c8в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
              r6c3=X and r6c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c6=X в†’ r9c3, r9c8в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              col 6 {[r3c6=r4c9], r8c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ [r3c6=r4c9]в‰ X
              col 8 {r5c8, r8c8, r9c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              box 6 {[r3c6=r4c9], r5c8, r6c7}: [r3c6=r4c9], r5c8в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
              r6c3=X and r6c7=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r4c9). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c8, r9c8, r9c3}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c8} and {r8c8, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c6, r8c8в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r9c3, r9c6в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r8c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r8c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
              r3c6=X в†’ r3c9, r4c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r7c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c3=X в†’ r9c6, r9c8в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X

              col 8 {r5c8, r8c8, r9c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
              r3c6=X в†’ r3c9, r4c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r7c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+ladder+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r8c3вЂ”r9c3, r8c6вЂ”r9c6, r8c8вЂ”r9c8}:
      Satellites r6c3 (rung 1), r3c6 (rung 2), r5c8 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r3c6, r5c8, r6c3, r6c7} (spine r5c8вЂ”r6c3).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c8, r9c8, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c8} and {r8c8, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c6, r8c8в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r9c3, r9c6в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
        col 6 {[r3c6=r6c7], r8c6, r9c6}: r8c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ [r3c6=r6c7]=X
        col 8 {r5c8, r8c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
        box 6 {[r3c6=r6c7], r4c9, r5c8}: [r3c6=r6c7]=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
        [r3c6=r6c7]=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r7c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c3=X в†’ r9c6, r9c8в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
        col 6 {[r3c6=r6c7], r8c6, r9c6}: r8c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ [r3c6=r6c7]=X
        col 8 {r5c8, r8c8, r9c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
        box 6 {[r3c6=r6c7], r4c9, r5c8}: [r3c6=r6c7]=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
        [r3c6=r6c7]=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r7c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r8c8, r9c8, r7c9} (spine r8c8вЂ”r9c8).

      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c7). Identify.

      в†’ color([r3c9=r6c7]) = color(r9c6). Identify.

      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.

      в†’ color([r5c8=r7c9]) = color(r9c3). Identify.

      в†’ color([r4c9=r9c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  7.  Kв‚„ on {[r4c9=r6c3=r9c8], [r5c8=r7c9=r9c3], r8c3, r8c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  2.  Guardian: r6c6 guards oddagon {r6c6, r6c3, r5c3, [r5c8=r9c7], r9c6} (source r9c3).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, [r6c6=r9c3]}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, r6c9} and {r3c9, [r6c6=r9c3]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r6c9) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, [r6c6=r9c3], r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r6c3, [r6c6=r9c3]в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, [r6c6=r9c3]}: r6c3, [r6c6=r9c3]в‰ X в†’ r5c3=X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r6c6=r9c3], r9c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r3c9, r4c9в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, [r5c8=r9c7], r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ [r5c8=r9c7]в‰ X
        row 9 {[r5c8=r9c7], [r6c6=r9c3], r9c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color([r6c6=r9c3]) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, [r6c6=r9c3], r6c9}: [r6c6=r9c3]=X в†’ r6c3, r6c9в‰ X
        row 9 {[r5c8=r9c7], [r6c6=r9c3], r9c6}: [r6c6=r9c3]=X в†’ [r5c8=r9c7], r9c6в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, [r6c6=r9c3]}: [r6c6=r9c3]=X в†’ r5c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r6c6=r9c3], r9c6}: [r6c6=r9c3]=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r6c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, [r5c8=r9c7], r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r4c9, r6c9, r5c8} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8], r7c8, r8c8, r9c7} (spine r7c8вЂ”r8c8).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r5c8]) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8=r9c7], r3c6, r9c6, r6c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r5c8=r9c7]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c6=r9c7], r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 8, col 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c9} = {r9c7}.
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r6c6, r3c6, [r3c9=r9c7], r9c3} (source r9c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c8, r6c9, [r6c3=r9c6]}:
      Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c9} and {r5c8, [r6c3=r9c6]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c9) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c6], r6c6, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ [r6c3=r9c6], r6c6в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c6], r9c3}: r5c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r6c3=r9c6], r6c6}: [r6c3=r9c6], r6c6в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c7], r4c9, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ [r3c9=r9c7], r4c9в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
        row 9 {[r3c9=r9c7], [r6c3=r9c6], r9c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c8) = color([r6c3=r9c6]) = X
        row 6 {[r6c3=r9c6], r6c6, r6c9}: [r6c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r6c6, r6c9в‰ X
        row 9 {[r3c9=r9c7], [r6c3=r9c6], r9c3}: [r6c3=r9c6]=X в†’ [r3c9=r9c7], r9c3в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, [r6c3=r9c6], r9c3}: [r6c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r5c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r6c3=r9c6], r6c6}: [r6c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X
        col 8 {r5c8, r7c8, r8c8}: r5c8=X в†’ r7c8, r8c8в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c7], r4c9, r6c9}: [r3c9=r9c7], r6c9в‰ X в†’ r4c9=X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c9}: r4c9=X and r5c8=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r7c8, r8c8, r9c7} (spine r7c8вЂ”r8c8).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {[r5c8=r9c7]вЂ”r6c9, r9c3вЂ”r6c3, r9c6вЂ”r6c6}:
      Satellites r4c9 (rung 1), r5c3 (rung 2), r3c6 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub r4c9 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, [r5c8=r9c7], r5c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      Diagonals: {[r3c6=r7c9], r6c2} and {[r4c9=r5c3], r6c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c6=r7c9]) = color(r6c2) = X

        box 7 {r7c3, r8c2, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X

  2.  Diamond {[r3c9=r6c8], r3c6, r6c6, r5c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r6c8]) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8], r5c3, r6c3} (spine [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8]вЂ”r5c3).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r4c9, [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8], [r5c9=r6c3], r5c3} (spine [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8]вЂ”[r5c9=r6c3]).
      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r5c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r4c9=r5c3], [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8], [r5c9=r6c3], r6c6} (spine [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8]вЂ”[r5c9=r6c3]).
      в†’ color([r4c9=r5c3]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8], [r4c9=r5c3=r6c6], [r5c9=r6c3]} (spine [r3c9=r5c6=r6c8]вЂ”[r4c9=r5c3=r6c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color([r5c9=r6c3]). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c6=r6c8], [r3c6=r5c9=r6c3], [r4c9=r5c3=r6c6], r9c3} (spine [r3c6=r5c9=r6c3]вЂ”[r4c9=r5c3=r6c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r5c6=r6c8]) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  8.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r5c6=r6c8=r9c3], r7c8, r8c8, r9c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 5, row 6}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6}.
      Remainder: {[r3c9=r6c8], r5c9} = {r3c6, r9c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r5c6, r5c3, r9c3}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, r5c3} and {r5c6, r9c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r5c3) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c3=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X в†’ r6c3, r9c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r6c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X
        r3c6=X в†’ [r3c9=r6c8]в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c8], r6c3, r6c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c6) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6=X в†’ r5c3, r5c9в‰ X
        col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r6c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r3c6, r6c6в‰ X
        r9c3=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c8], r6c3, r6c6}: r6c3, r6c6в‰ X в†’ [r3c9=r6c8]=X
        col 8 {[r3c9=r6c8], r7c8, r8c8}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X в†’ r7c8, r8c8в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c8], r4c9, r5c9}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c8, r8c8, r9c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c8, r7c8, r8c8, r9c7} (spine r7c8вЂ”r8c8).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r5c3вЂ”r6c3, r5c6вЂ”r6c6, r5c9вЂ”[r6c8=r9c7]}:
      Satellites r9c3 (rung 1), r3c6 (rung 2), r4c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub r4c9 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, [r6c8=r9c7], r9c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r6c8:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        2.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r6c8], r3c9, r4c9, r5c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r6c8). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r4c9, r6c8}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r4c9} and {r3c9, r6c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r4c9) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r5c6, r9c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9}: r4c9=X в†’ r3c9, r5c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r4c9=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c6, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r5c3=X
              col 3 {r5c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r5c3=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 4 {r5c3, r6c1, r6c2}: r5c3=X в†’ r6c1, r6c2в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              row 6 {r6c1, r6c2, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c1, r6c2, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c1, r6c2в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
              box 4 {r5c3, r6c1, r6c2}: r6c1, r6c2в‰ X в†’ r5c3=X
              r3c9=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r5c3=X в†’ r5c6, r7c3, r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r5c6в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              r9c2=X and r9c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r4c9, r6c9, r5c8} (spine r4c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8], r3c6, r5c6, r9c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”r5c6).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r5c8]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c6, [r3c9=r5c8=r9c6], r5c3, r9c3} (spine [r3c9=r5c8=r9c6]вЂ”r5c3).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c9=r5c8=r9c6], [r5c6=r9c3], r5c3} (spine [r3c9=r5c8=r9c6]вЂ”[r5c6=r9c3]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r5c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r5c8=r9c6], [r3c6=r5c3], [r5c6=r9c3], r6c3} (spine [r3c6=r5c3]вЂ”[r5c6=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r5c8=r9c6]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r5c8=r6c3=r9c6], r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r5c3:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r5c3). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r5c3], r3c9, r4c9, r5c8}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r5c3], r4c9} and {r3c9, r5c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r5c3]) = color(r4c9) = X
              row 5 {[r3c6=r5c3], r5c6, r5c8}: [r3c6=r5c3]=X в†’ r5c6, r5c8в‰ X
              col 3 {[r3c6=r5c3], r6c3, r9c3}: [r3c6=r5c3]=X в†’ r6c3, r9c3в‰ X
              col 6 {[r3c6=r5c3], r5c6, r9c6}: [r3c6=r5c3]=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r6c9}: r4c9=X в†’ r3c9, r6c9в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c3, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r6c5=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r5c8) = X
              row 5 {[r3c6=r5c3], r5c6, r5c8}: r5c8=X в†’ [r3c6=r5c3], r5c6в‰ X
              col 6 {[r3c6=r5c3], r5c6, r9c6}: [r3c6=r5c3], r5c6в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r6c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r4c9, r6c9в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
              r9c6=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 3 {[r3c6=r5c3], r6c3, r9c3}: [r3c6=r5c3], r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r7c5, r8c5в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              r6c3=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r5c3). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c3, r5c8, r6c9, r6c3}:
            Diagonals: {r5c3, r6c9} and {r5c8, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c3) = color(r6c9) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c3=X в†’ r5c6, r5c8в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r6c3, r6c5в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r5c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r3c9, r4c9в‰ X
              r5c3=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r5c6в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r5c8) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 5 {r5c3, r5c6, r5c8}: r5c8=X в†’ r5c3, r5c6в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c5, r6c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r5c3, r6c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c9}: r5c8=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r6c9}: r4c9, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              r3c9=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r5c6в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r7c9, r8c7, r8c8, r8c3} (spine r8c7вЂ”r8c8).
      в†’ color(r7c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.

  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r7c9=r8c3=r9c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r7c9=r8c3=r9c6]). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c6вЂ”r6c6, r3c8вЂ”r6c8, r3c9вЂ”r6c9}:
      Satellites r9c6 (rung 1), r8c8 (rung 2), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c6} = {r7c9}.
      в†’ color(r9c6) = color(r7c9), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c7} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c9, [r8c3=r9c7], r8c8, r8c6} (spine [r8c3=r9c7]вЂ”r8c8).
      в†’ color(r7c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r7c9=r8c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r7c9=r8c6], r6c6, r6c8, r6c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c7} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 6, col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r8c3=r9c7]} = {r7c9}.
      в†’ color([r8c3=r9c7]) = color(r7c9), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r3c8=X в†’ r7c8в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c8, r7c9, r9c7}: r7c8, r7c9в‰ X в†’ r9c7=X
        r8c6=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        r9c7=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c6, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c6, r6c9в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r3c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r8c6=X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r7c8в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c8, r7c9, r9c7}: r7c8, r7c9в‰ X в†’ r9c7=X
        r8c6=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        r9c7=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c7} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r3c6вЂ”r6c6, r3c8вЂ”r6c8, r3c9вЂ”r6c9}:
      Satellites r8c6 (rung 1), r7c8 (rung 2), r7c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Kв‚„ on {r7c8, r7c9, [r8c3=r9c7], r8c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set) ===

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 6, col 8, col 9}.

      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.

      в†’ color(r8c9) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r8c3, [r8c5=r9c8], [r8c9=r9c2], r9c5} (spine [r8c5=r9c8]вЂ”[r8c9=r9c2]).

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c6вЂ”r6c6, r3c8вЂ”r6c8, r3c9вЂ”r6c9}:
      Satellites r7c6 (rung 1), r9c8 (rung 2), r8c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r8c9, r7c6, r8c5, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c5).
      в†’ color(r8c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c9=r9c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r7c6, r8c5, r8c3, r9c2, r9c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  3.  Diamond {r8c9, r8c3, [r8c5=r9c8], r9c2} (spine r8c3вЂ”[r8c5=r9c8]).
      в†’ color(r8c9) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r8c3, [r8c5=r9c8], [r8c9=r9c2], r9c4} (spine [r8c5=r9c8]вЂ”[r8c9=r9c2]).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r7c6, [r8c3=r9c4], [r8c5=r9c8], [r8c9=r9c2]} (spine [r8c3=r9c4]вЂ”[r8c5=r9c8]).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color([r8c9=r9c2]). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r7c6=r8c9=r9c2]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r7c6=r8c9=r9c2]). Identify.
  7.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r7c6=r8c9=r9c2], r6c6, r6c8, r6c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c6вЂ”r6c6, r3c8вЂ”r6c8, r3c9вЂ”r6c9}:
      Satellites r7c6 (rung 1), r9c8 (rung 2), r8c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r8c9, r7c6, r8c5, r9c4} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c5).
      в†’ color(r8c9) = color(r9c4). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c9=r9c4] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r7c6, r8c5, r8c3, r9c2, r9c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r6c9) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X

      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 6, col 8, col 9}.

      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.

        r9c5=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c5} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c6, [r8c3=r9c5], r8c5, r8c9} (spine [r8c3=r9c5]вЂ”r8c5).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r7c6=r8c9]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r7c6=r8c9]). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r7c6=r8c9], r6c6, r6c8, r6c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c6вЂ”r6c6, r3c8вЂ”r6c8, r3c9вЂ”r6c9}:
      Satellites r7c6 (rung 1), r8c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r8c9} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color(r8c9) = color(r7c6), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c3, r8c8:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c8, [r6c3=r8c8], r8c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, [r6c3=r8c8]} and {r3c8, r8c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color([r6c3=r8c8]) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c8, r3c9в‰ X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r8c8], r6c8, r6c9}: [r6c3=r8c8]=X в†’ r6c8, r6c9в‰ X
              row 8 {[r6c3=r8c8], r8c3, r8c6}: [r6c3=r8c8]=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
              col 3 {[r6c3=r8c8], r8c3, r9c3}: [r6c3=r8c8]=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r3c9, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r7c9=X
              box 9 {[r6c3=r8c8], r7c9, r9c7}: [r6c3=r8c8]=X and r7c9=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c8) = color(r8c6) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 8 {[r6c3=r8c8], r8c3, r8c6}: r8c6=X в†’ [r6c3=r8c8], r8c3в‰ X
              col 3 {[r6c3=r8c8], r8c3, r9c3}: [r6c3=r8c8], r8c3в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
              col 8 {r3c8, [r6c3=r8c8], r6c8}: r3c8=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X
              r8c6=X в†’ r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c3=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r6c3=r8c8], r6c8, r6c9}: [r6c3=r8c8], r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c9=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
              box 9 {[r6c3=r8c8], r7c9, r9c7}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) в‰  color(r8c8). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r3c9, r7c9, r8c8}:
            Diagonals: {r3c8, r7c9} and {r3c9, r8c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r7c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r3c8=X в†’ r6c8, r8c8в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X в†’ r6c9в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r8c3, r9c3в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3, r8c8в‰ X в†’ r8c6=X
              row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c7}: r9c3, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r9c5=X
              r8c6=X and r9c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r6c9, r7c9в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X
              r8c8=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8вЂ”r3c9, r6c8вЂ”r6c9, r8c8вЂ”r7c9}:
      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 2), r9c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Diamond {r8c3, r6c3, r9c3, r9c7} (spine r6c3вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r8c3=r9c7] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r6c3, r9c3, r9c5, r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r9c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c6, [r3c6=r9c5], r9c6, r9c3} (spine [r3c6=r9c5]вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r3c6=r9c5]} = {[r7c6=r9c3]}.
      в†’ color([r3c6=r9c5]) = color([r7c6=r9c3]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set) ===

      в†’ color(r9c5) = color(r7c6), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        box 8 {r7c6, r9c4, r9c5}: r9c4, r9c5в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        r3c6=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r8c8) = X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r6c3, r6c8в‰ X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c8, r8c9}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r3c8в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X
        r9c3=X в†’ r9c4, r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
        box 8 {r7c6, r9c4, r9c5}: r9c4, r9c5в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        r3c6=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r7c6, r9c4, r9c5, r9c3} (spine r9c4вЂ”r9c5).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c6} = {[r7c6=r9c3]}.
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color([r7c6=r9c3]), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+parity) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r9c5], r3c8, r9c8} (spine [r3c6=r9c5]вЂ”r3c8).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, [r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r9c8], r9c2} (spine [r3c6=r9c5]вЂ”[r3c9=r9c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r3c6=r9c5], [r3c8=r9c2], [r3c9=r9c8], r6c8} (spine [r3c8=r9c2]вЂ”[r3c9=r9c8]).
      в†’ color([r3c6=r9c5]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  5.  Parity transport:
      row 3 {[r3c6=r6c8=r9c5], [r3c8=r9c2], [r3c9=r9c8]}
      row 6 {r6c3, [r3c6=r6c8=r9c5], r6c9}
      row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}
      row 9 {[r3c8=r9c2], [r3c6=r6c8=r9c5], [r3c9=r9c8]}
      Parallel links: row 3в†”row 6 (box, col, col); row 6в†”row 8 (col, box, col); row 8в†”row 9 (box, box, box)
      в†’ same permutation parity.
      Each pair shares a column в†’ distinct permutations.
      4 same-parity permutations from 3 available в†’ pigeonhole contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r8c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r8c9], r6c9, r6c3, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r8c9], r6c3} and {r6c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r8c9]) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 3 {[r3c6=r8c9], r3c8, r3c9}: [r3c6=r8c9]=X в†’ r3c8, r3c9в‰ X

              r9c5=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              [r3c6=r8c9]=X and r9c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r8c3) = X

              col 6 {[r3c6=r8c9], r7c6, r8c6}: [r3c6=r8c9], r8c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              col 9 {[r3c6=r8c9], r3c9, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c6, r9c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r9c5в‰ X
              r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {[r3c6=r8c9], r3c8, r3c9}: [r3c6=r8c9], r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c8=X

              Contradiction.

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c8, r6c9, r8c9, r9c8}:
            Diagonals: {r6c8, r8c9} and {r6c9, r9c8} (non-adjacent).

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (ladder+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c8вЂ”r3c9, r6c8вЂ”r6c9, r9c8вЂ”r9c9}:
      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 2), r9c2 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Guardian: r6c3 guards oddagon {r6c3, r3c6, r7c6, r8c5, r8c3} (source r8c6).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c3=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r7c6, r8c5, r8c3, r9c2} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_ladder] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c3, r8c6:
      Case A: color(r6c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r3c9, r6c9, r6c8}:
            Diagonals: {r3c8, r6c9} and {r3c9, r6c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r6c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r8c6], r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ [r6c3=r8c6], r6c8в‰ X

              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X
              box 8 {[r6c3=r8c6], r7c6, r8c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X
              row 8 {[r6c3=r8c6], r8c3, r8c5}: [r6c3=r8c6], r8c5в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c8, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              r8c3=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
              row 6 {[r6c3=r8c6], r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8=X в†’ [r6c3=r8c6], r6c9в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, [r6c3=r8c6], r7c6}: r3c6, [r6c3=r8c6]в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r9c8в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X
              box 8 {[r6c3=r8c6], r7c6, r8c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X
              row 8 {[r6c3=r8c6], r8c3, r8c5}: [r6c3=r8c6], r8c5в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c8, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              r8c3=X and r9c2=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c3) в‰  color(r8c6). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r3c9, r9c9, r9c8}:
            Diagonals: {r3c8, r9c9} and {r3c9, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r9c9) = X

              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c2, r9c8в‰ X

              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c6}: r8c3, r8c6в‰ X в†’ r8c5=X

      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 2), r9c2 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  2.  Branch on r3c6, r8c3:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        3.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r8c3], r7c6, r8c4, r8c5}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r8c3). Add edge.
        4.  Kв‚„ on {r3c6, r6c3, r8c3, r9c2}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5, r8c3} (spine r8c4вЂ”r8c5).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r3c6, r6c8:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r6c8], [r7c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c8}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r6c8], r9c2} and {[r7c6=r8c3], r9c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r6c8]) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 3 {[r3c6=r6c8], r3c8, r3c9}: [r3c6=r6c8]=X в†’ r3c8, r3c9в‰ X
              row 6 {[r3c6=r6c8], r6c3, r6c9}: [r3c6=r6c8]=X в†’ r6c3, r6c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c8, r9c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r7c6=r8c3]) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 8 {[r7c6=r8c3], r8c4, r8c5}: [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r8c4, r8c5в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c8=X в†’ r9c2, r9c9в‰ X
              col 8 {[r3c6=r6c8], r3c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ [r3c6=r6c8], r3c8в‰ X
              [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {[r3c6=r6c8], r3c8, r3c9}: [r3c6=r6c8], r3c8в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              row 6 {[r3c6=r6c8], r6c3, r6c9}: [r3c6=r6c8], r6c3в‰ X в†’ r6c9=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X and r6c9=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r6c8). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r6c8, r6c3, [r7c6=r8c3]}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r6c3} and {r6c8, [r7c6=r8c3]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c8, r3c9в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c8, r6c9в‰ X
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r3c8, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r9c8=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X
              r3c6=X в†’ [r7c6=r8c3]в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c8=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c8=X and r9c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c8) = color([r7c6=r8c3]) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c3, r6c9в‰ X
              row 8 {[r7c6=r8c3], r8c4, r8c5}: [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r8c4, r8c5в‰ X
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r3c8, r9c8в‰ X
              r6c8=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c6, r3c8в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c8в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X and r9c9=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.

      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9, [r6c8=r9c9]} (spine r3c8вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color([r6c8=r9c9]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c8, [r3c6=r6c8=r9c9], r3c9, r6c9} (spine [r3c6=r6c8=r9c9]вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r6c9). Identify.

  6.  Diamond {[r3c8=r6c9], [r3c6=r6c8=r9c9], [r3c9=r6c3=r9c2], r9c3} (spine [r3c6=r6c8=r9c9]вЂ”[r3c9=r6c3=r9c2]).

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.

      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_set] (diamond+xwing+guardian) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3вЂ”r9c3).

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3, r9c6} (spine r9c2вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r3c6вЂ”r3c9, r6c6вЂ”r6c9, [r8c3=r9c6]вЂ”r8c9}:
      Satellites r3c8 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 2), r8c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub r8c7 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c8, r6c3, [r8c3=r9c6], r8c9, r7c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c6} = {r7c8}.
      в†’ color(r9c6) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r6c9 guards oddagon {r6c9, r3c9, r3c8, [r7c8=r9c6], r6c6} (source r3c6).
      в†’ color(r6c9) = color(r3c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c3, r8c3, r8c9}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c6=r6c9], r8c3} and {r6c3, r8c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c6=r6c9]) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 3 {[r3c6=r6c9], r3c8, r3c9}: [r3c6=r6c9]=X в†’ r3c8, r3c9в‰ X
        row 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c3, r6c6}: [r3c6=r6c9]=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c9}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c7, r8c9в‰ X
        col 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c6, [r7c8=r9c6]}: [r3c6=r6c9]=X в†’ [r7c8=r9c6]в‰ X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
        box 9 {[r7c8=r9c6], r8c7, r8c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c3) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c3, r6c6}: r6c3=X в†’ [r3c6=r6c9], r6c6в‰ X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c7, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c7в‰ X
        col 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c6, [r7c8=r9c6]}: [r3c6=r6c9], r6c6в‰ X в†’ [r7c8=r9c6]=X
        col 9 {[r3c6=r6c9], r3c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X
        box 9 {[r7c8=r9c6], r8c7, r8c9}: [r7c8=r9c6]=X and r8c9=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c6} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r3c6вЂ”r3c9, r6c6вЂ”r6c9, [r8c3=r9c6]вЂ”r8c9}:
      Satellites r3c8 (rung 1), r6c3 (rung 2), r8c7 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub r8c7 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c8, r6c3, [r8c3=r9c6], r8c9, r7c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c6, r3c8в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: r6c3, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r6c9=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r3c9=X and r6c9=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c3) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c5в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c4, r9c8}: r9c3=X в†’ r9c4, r9c8в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X

        r7c6=X в†’ r3c6, r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c6, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c8=X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c8, r8c5} = {r7c6, r9c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Diamond {r8c5, r7c6, r9c4, r9c3} (spine r7c6вЂ”r9c4).
      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c3, r8c3, [r8c5=r9c3], r8c9} (spine r8c3вЂ”[r8c5=r9c3]).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, [r6c3=r8c9], r6c9, r6c6} (spine [r6c3=r8c9]вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, [r3c9=r6c6], r7c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r3c9=r6c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r7c6], [r8c5=r9c3], r9c4, r9c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

              r7c6=X в†’ r3c6, r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X в†’ [r3c8=r6c3]в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c6, [r3c8=r6c3], r3c9}: r3c6, [r3c8=r6c3]в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              row 6 {[r3c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c9}: [r3c8=r6c3], r6c6в‰ X в†’ r6c9=X

      в†’ color(r3c9) = color([r7c6=r8c3=r9c8]). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r7c6=r8c3=r9c8], [r6c3=r9c2], r6c6, r6c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c8, r6c3:
      Case A: color(r3c8) = color(r6c3). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c8=r6c3], r8c3, r9c1, r9c8}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c8=r6c3], r9c1} and {r8c3, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c8=r6c3]) = color(r9c1) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, [r3c8=r6c3], r3c9}: [r3c8=r6c3]=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 6 {[r3c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c9}: [r3c8=r6c3]=X в†’ r6c6, r6c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c8}: r9c1=X в†’ r9c2, r9c8в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r3c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c4, r8c5в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c4, r8c5в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c4, r8c5в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              r7c6=X в†’ r3c6, r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X в†’ [r3c8=r6c3]в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c6, [r3c8=r6c3], r3c9}: r3c6, [r3c8=r6c3]в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              row 6 {[r3c8=r6c3], r6c6, r6c9}: [r3c8=r6c3], r6c6в‰ X в†’ r6c9=X
              r3c9=X and r6c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c8) в‰  color(r6c3). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r3c9, r6c9, r6c3}:
            Diagonals: {r3c8, r6c9} and {r3c9, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r6c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r3c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r8c4, r8c5в‰ X
              r3c8=X в†’ r9c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c4, r8c5в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c9в‰ X

              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X

      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r7c6=r9c8], [r3c6=r6c9], [r3c8=r6c6], r6c3} (spine [r3c6=r6c9]вЂ”[r3c8=r6c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r7c6=r9c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r6c3=r7c6=r9c8], r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (set) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 7, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c2} = {r8c3}.
      в†’ color(r9c2) = color(r8c3), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r7c6=r8c3]) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 7 {[r7c6=r8c3], r7c7, r7c8}: [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r7c7, r7c8в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r9c2, r9c3в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, [r7c6=r8c3], r9c3}: [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, [r7c6=r8c3]}: [r7c6=r8c3]=X в†’ r3c6, r6c6в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, r7c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r3c8в‰ X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c6, r3c8в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: r6c3, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r6c9=X
        r3c9=X and r6c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c7) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 7 {[r7c6=r8c3], r7c7, r7c8}: r7c7=X в†’ [r7c6=r8c3], r7c8в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c8}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c3, r9c8в‰ X
        col 3 {r6c3, [r7c6=r8c3], r9c3}: [r7c6=r8c3], r9c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        col 8 {r3c8, r7c8, r9c8}: r7c8, r9c8в‰ X в†’ r3c8=X
        r3c8=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, [r7c6=r8c3]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r7c6, r7c7, r7c8, r9c8} (spine r7c7вЂ”r7c8).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, r3c8, [r7c6=r9c8]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c8).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color([r7c6=r9c8]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, [r3c9=r7c6=r9c8], r6c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r3c9=r7c6=r9c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c8=r6c6], [r3c9=r7c6=r9c8], r6c9} (spine [r3c8=r6c6]вЂ”[r3c9=r7c6=r9c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r7c6=r9c8], [r3c6=r6c9], [r3c8=r6c6], r6c3} (spine [r3c6=r6c9]вЂ”[r3c8=r6c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r7c6=r9c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r6c3=r7c6=r9c8], r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+hexagon) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 7, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r7c6} = {r8c3}.
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c9, r6c3, r6c6, [r7c6=r8c3]} (spine r6c3вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c9) = color([r7c6=r8c3]). Identify.
  3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c6, r3c8, r9c8, r9c3, r6c3, r6c6}
      Bridges: r3c9вЂ”[r6c9=r7c6=r8c3] (edges r3c6вЂ”r3c8 в†” r9c3вЂ”r6c3), r7c8вЂ”[r6c9=r7c6=r8c3] (edges r3c8вЂ”r9c8 в†” r6c3вЂ”r6c6), r9c2вЂ”[r6c9=r7c6=r8c3] (edges r9c8вЂ”r9c3 в†” r6c6вЂ”r3c6).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of Cв‚† requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 7, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r7c8} = {r8c3}.
      в†’ color(r7c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  2.  Branch on [r7c8=r8c3], r9c6:
      Case A: color([r7c8=r8c3]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
        3.  Guardian: r6c9 guards oddagon {r6c9, r3c9, r3c8, [r7c8=r8c3=r9c6], r6c6} (source r3c6).
            в†’ color(r6c9) = color(r3c6). Identify.
        4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c3, r6c6, [r7c8=r8c3=r9c6]}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r7c8=r8c3]) в‰  color(r9c6). Add edge.
        5.  Kв‚„ on {r7c4, r7c5, [r7c8=r8c3], r9c6}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  3.  Diamond {r6c9, r6c3, r6c6, [r7c3=r9c6]} (spine r6c3вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color(r6c9) = color([r7c3=r9c6]). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c8, [r8c2=r9c8], [r6c9=r7c3=r9c6]}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, [r8c2=r9c8]} and {r3c8, [r6c9=r7c3=r9c6]} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color([r8c2=r9c8]) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c8, r3c9в‰ X
        row 8 {[r8c2=r9c8], r8c8, r8c9}: [r8c2=r9c8]=X в†’ r8c8, r8c9в‰ X
        row 9 {[r6c9=r7c3=r9c6], [r8c2=r9c8], r9c2}: [r8c2=r9c8]=X в†’ [r6c9=r7c3=r9c6], r9c2в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, [r6c9=r7c3=r9c6]}: r3c6=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r7c3=r9c6], r8c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c8) = color([r6c9=r7c3=r9c6]) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, [r6c9=r7c3=r9c6]}: [r6c9=r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
        row 9 {[r6c9=r7c3=r9c6], [r8c2=r9c8], r9c2}: [r6c9=r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ [r8c2=r9c8], r9c2в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, [r8c2=r9c8], r8c8}: r3c8=X в†’ r8c8в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r7c3=r9c6], r8c9}: [r6c9=r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X
        box 9 {[r8c2=r9c8], r8c8, r8c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c2, r8c8, r8c9, r9c8} (spine r8c8вЂ”r8c9).
      в†’ color(r8c2) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 6, col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, [r8c2=r9c8]} = {[r8c2=r9c8], r9c6}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Guardian: r6c6 guards oddagon {r6c6, r6c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c6} (source [r8c2=r9c8]).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color([r8c2=r9c8]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c6=r8c2=r9c8] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c8, r8c8, r8c9, r6c9, r6c3, r7c3, r9c2, r9c6} (length 9) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c6} = {r7c3}.
      в†’ color(r9c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 6}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c8, r6c3} = {[r7c3=r9c6], r8c9}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r3c9, r6c9, r6c3}:
      Diagonals: {r3c8, r6c9} and {r3c9, r6c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r6c9) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r6c3, r6c6в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, [r7c3=r9c6]}: r3c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ [r7c3=r9c6]=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X
        box 7 {[r7c3=r9c6], r8c2, r9c2}: [r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r8c2, r9c2в‰ X
        r3c8=X в†’ r8c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        [r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c2, r8c8, r8c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c3) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c9в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, [r7c3=r9c6]}: r3c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ [r7c3=r9c6]=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X
        box 7 {[r7c3=r9c6], r8c2, r9c2}: [r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r8c2, r9c2в‰ X
        [r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X (adjacent)
        r6c3=X and [r7c3=r9c6]=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X

        r3c8=X в†’ r8c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        [r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 8 {r8c2, r8c8, r8c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c3) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c3, r6c6, r6c9}: r6c3=X в†’ r6c6, r6c9в‰ X

        [r7c3=r9c6]=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X (adjacent)
        r6c3=X and [r7c3=r9c6]=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        col 3 {[r6c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c8=r6c9=r9c6], r3c9, r8c9}: [r3c8=r6c9=r9c6]=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X
        [r3c8=r6c9=r9c6]=X в†’ r7c8, r8c5в‰ X (adjacent)

      Case 2: color([r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r7c8) = X

        r7c8=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X (adjacent)

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c9, [r6c3=r9c2], r6c6, r9c6} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]вЂ”r6c6).

      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r6c9=r9c6]). Identify.

      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.

      в†’ color([r3c6=r6c3=r9c2]) = color(r8c9). Identify.

      в†’ color([r3c9=r6c6]) = color(r7c8). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+oddwheel+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c8, r8c5} = {r7c3, r9c6}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Guardian: r6c6 guards oddagon {r6c6, r6c3, r8c3, r9c2, r9c6} (source r7c3).
      в†’ color(r6c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  3.  Guardian: r3c9 guards oddagon {r3c9, r3c6, r9c6, r9c2, r8c3, r6c3, r6c9} (source [r6c6=r7c3]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color([r6c6=r7c3]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c9=r6c6=r7c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c8, r7c8, r8c9, r8c3, r9c2, r9c6} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c8, r7c9, r8c7}: r7c8, r7c9в‰ X в†’ r8c7=X
        r8c7=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 6 {r3c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r8c6в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
        r9c6=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r7c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r7c8в‰ X

        r9c6=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c6} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c6, [r8c3=r9c6], r8c6, r8c7} (spine [r8c3=r9c6]вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r8c7). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r8c7], r3c8, r7c8} (spine [r3c6=r8c7]вЂ”r3c8).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r7c8). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c8, [r3c6=r8c7], [r3c9=r7c8], r7c9} (spine [r3c6=r8c7]вЂ”[r3c9=r7c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r7c9], [r3c9=r7c8], r5c9, r6c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        col 6 {r3c6, [r7c6=r8c8], r8c6}: [r7c6=r8c8]=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, [r7c6=r8c8]}: [r7c6=r8c8]=X в†’ r3c8, r6c8в‰ X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c6, r3c8в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r5c9, r6c9в‰ X
        box 6 {r5c9, r6c8, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c5) = X
        row 8 {[r7c6=r8c8], r8c3, r8c6}: r8c3=X в†’ [r7c6=r8c8], r8c6в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c5}: r9c5=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r7c6=r8c8], r8c6}: [r7c6=r8c8], r8c6в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
        r3c6=X в†’ r3c8, r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, [r7c6=r8c8]}: r3c8, [r7c6=r8c8]в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
        box 6 {r5c9, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8=X в†’ r5c9, r6c9в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9, r6c8} (spine r5c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c6, r3c8, [r3c9=r6c8], r8c8} (spine r3c8вЂ”[r3c9=r6c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c6, [r3c6=r8c8], r8c6, r8c3} (spine [r3c6=r8c8]вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r7c6=r8c3], r9c1, r9c2, r9c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        row 9 {[r7c6=r9c8], r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1=X в†’ [r7c6=r9c8], r9c2в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r7c6=r9c8], r8c6}: [r7c6=r9c8], r8c6в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
        r3c6=X в†’ r3c8, r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, [r7c6=r9c8]}: r3c8, [r7c6=r9c8]в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
        box 6 {r5c9, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8=X в†’ r5c9, r6c9в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9, r6c8} (spine r5c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c6, r3c8, [r3c9=r6c8], r9c8} (spine r3c8вЂ”[r3c9=r6c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c6=r9c8], r7c6, r8c6, r8c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color([r3c6=r9c8]) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r8c5=r9c8], r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      Remainder: {[r3c6=r9c8]} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color([r3c6=r9c8]) = color(r7c6), but they share a house. Contradiction.

  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r7c9=r8c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r3c8=r7c9=r8c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r3c8=r7c9=r8c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r7c9=r8c6], [r3c9=r6c6], r5c9, r6c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_ladder] (diamond+set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c9}.
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r7c9=r8c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c8=r7c9=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r6c8, r6c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Circular ladder {r3c6вЂ”r3c8, r6c6вЂ”r6c8, r8c6вЂ”r8c8}:
      Satellites r3c9 (rung 1), r8c3 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add edge.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r8c3, r8c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, r8c3} and {r3c9, r8c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c8, r3c9в‰ X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c6, r8c8в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r3c8, r8c8в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
        r6c8=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c7}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r9c7=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r3c9, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r7c9=X
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9=X and r9c7=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c6=X в†’ r8c3, r8c8в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r3c8, r8c8в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r5c9, r7c9в‰ X
        box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9, r8c8в‰ X в†’ r9c7=X
        r9c7=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r7c9=r8c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).

  4.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r3c8=r7c9=r8c6], r6c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r3c8=r7c9=r8c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r7c9=r8c6], [r3c9=r6c6], r5c9, r6c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 9}.
      Remainder: {r8c6} = {r7c9}.
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r6c8 guards oddagon {r6c8, r3c8, r3c9, [r7c9=r8c6], r6c6} (source r3c6).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r3c6). Identify.
  3.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r6c8], r3c9, r5c9, [r7c9=r8c6]}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_set] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c7} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r7c9, [r8c3=r9c7], r8c7, r8c6} (spine [r8c3=r9c7]вЂ”r8c7).
      в†’ color(r7c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r7c9=r8c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c8=r7c9=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r6c8, r6c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r7c6=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3, r9c9} (spine r9c2вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color([r7c6=r8c3]) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r7c6=r8c3=r9c9]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r7c6=r8c3=r9c9]). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c8=r7c6=r8c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r6c8, r6c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        r6c7=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
        r9c6=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r5c9=r6c6]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], r9c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], [r3c9=r9c6], r8c9} (spine [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”[r3c9=r9c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c6], [r3c6=r8c9], [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], r8c8} (spine [r3c6=r8c9]вЂ”[r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r8c8=r9c6], r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r5c9=r6c6], r9c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r5c9=r6c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, [r3c9=r9c6], r7c9, r8c8}:
      Diagonals: {r3c8, r7c9} and {[r3c9=r9c6], r8c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r7c9) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, [r3c9=r9c6]}: r3c8=X в†’ r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6]в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c6]}: r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6]в‰ X в†’ [r5c9=r6c6]=X

        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c6], r7c9}: [r5c9=r6c6]=X and r7c9=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r8c8) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, [r3c9=r9c6]}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
        row 9 {[r3c9=r9c6], r9c2, r9c3}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ r9c2, r9c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c6]}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ [r5c9=r6c6]в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c6], r7c9}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
        box 6 {[r5c9=r6c6], r6c7, r6c8}: [r5c9=r6c6], r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r9c2, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
        r8c3=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r5c9=r6c6]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3, r9c6} (spine r9c2вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r7c9, r8c8, [r8c3=r9c6]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        row 6 {r6c6, r6c7, r6c8}: r6c7=X в†’ r6c6, r6c8в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r3c8, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r8c8=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r5c9, r7c9в‰ X
        r8c8=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        r9c6=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r5c9=r6c6]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], r9c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], [r3c9=r9c6], r7c9} (spine [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”[r3c9=r9c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c6], [r3c6=r7c9], [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], r8c8} (spine [r3c6=r7c9]вЂ”[r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r8c8=r9c6], r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r5c9=r6c6], r9c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r5c9=r6c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, [r3c9=r9c6], r7c9, r8c8}:
      Diagonals: {r3c8, r7c9} and {[r3c9=r9c6], r8c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r7c9) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, [r3c9=r9c6]}: r3c8=X в†’ r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6]в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c6]}: r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6]в‰ X в†’ [r5c9=r6c6]=X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r3c8=X в†’ r6c8, r8c8в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c6], r7c9}: [r5c9=r6c6]=X and r7c9=X clash
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r8c8) = X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, [r3c9=r9c6]}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
        row 9 {[r3c9=r9c6], r9c1, r9c2}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c6]}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ [r5c9=r6c6]в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c6], r7c9}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
        box 6 {[r5c9=r6c6], r6c7, r6c8}: [r5c9=r6c6], r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
        r8c3=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c6, r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r5c9=r6c6]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c6} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r7c9, r8c8, [r8c3=r9c6]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c9, r6c8, r9c8, r8c9}:
      Diagonals: {r5c9, r9c8} and {r6c8, r8c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r5c9) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 9 {r9c3, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r9c3, r9c5в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r3c8, r6c8в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r3c9, r8c9в‰ X
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
        col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r7c6, r8c6в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c6, r9c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r6c8) = color(r8c9) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r3c8, r9c8в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r3c9, r5c9в‰ X
        r6c8=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
        col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r6c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r9c3=X
        col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c6, r9c5}: r7c6, r8c6в‰ X в†’ r9c5=X
        r9c3=X and r9c5=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r9c5], r3c8, r9c8} (spine [r3c6=r9c5]вЂ”r3c8).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, [r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r9c8], r9c3} (spine [r3c6=r9c5]вЂ”[r3c9=r9c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r3c6=r9c5], [r3c8=r9c3], [r3c9=r9c8], r6c8} (spine [r3c8=r9c3]вЂ”[r3c9=r9c8]).
      в†’ color([r3c6=r9c5]) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c8], [r3c6=r6c8=r9c5], [r3c8=r9c3], r6c3} (spine [r3c6=r6c8=r9c5]вЂ”[r3c8=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r9c8]) = color(r6c3). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r3c8=r9c3], [r3c6=r6c8=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c3=r9c8], r5c9} (spine [r3c6=r6c8=r9c5]вЂ”[r3c9=r6c3=r9c8]).
      в†’ color([r3c8=r9c3]) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  7.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r5c9=r9c3], [r3c9=r6c3=r9c8], r8c3, r8c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r9c5], r9c9, r9c2} (spine [r3c6=r9c5]вЂ”r9c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, [r3c9=r9c2], r5c9, r6c8}:
      Diagonals: {r3c8, r5c9} and {[r3c9=r9c2], r6c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r5c9) = X
        row 3 {[r3c6=r9c5], r3c8, [r3c9=r9c2]}: r3c8=X в†’ [r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r9c2]в‰ X

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r3c9=r9c2]) = color(r6c8) = X

        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c2], r5c9, r9c9}: [r3c9=r9c2]=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X

        r6c8=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.

      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, [r3c6=r9c5], r3c9, r9c9} (spine [r3c6=r9c5]вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, [r3c6=r9c5], r3c9, r9c9} (spine [r3c6=r9c5]вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c8=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c6=r9c5], r3c9, r5c9, r6c8, [r6c3=r9c2]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond+ladder) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3, r9c6} (spine r9c2вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c6). Identify.

      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), r5c8 (rung 2), r5c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Kв‚„ on {r5c8, r5c9, r6c6, r6c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  2.  Circular ladder {r3c6вЂ”r8c6, r3c8вЂ”[r8c3=r9c8], r3c9вЂ”r8c9}:
      Satellites r6c6 (rung 1), r5c8 (rung 2), r5c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Kв‚„ on {r5c8, r5c9, r6c6, r6c7}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў
