      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r3c3=r9c2], [r3c6=r9c7], [r3c9=r9c6], r6c6} (spine [r3c6=r9c7]вЂ”[r3c9=r9c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c3=r9c2]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c3=r6c6=r9c2], [r3c6=r9c7], [r3c9=r9c6], r7c9} (spine [r3c6=r9c7]вЂ”[r3c9=r9c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c3=r6c6=r9c2]) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c6], [r3c3=r6c6=r7c9=r9c2], [r3c6=r9c7], r8c8} (spine [r3c3=r6c6=r7c9=r9c2]вЂ”[r3c6=r9c7]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  7.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c3=r6c6=r7c9=r9c2], [r3c9=r8c8=r9c6], r5c9, r6c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c9, r8c8:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, [r3c9=r8c8], r6c8, r6c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r6c8} and {[r3c9=r8c8], r6c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r8c8]}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c3, [r3c9=r8c8]в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r6c6, r9c6в‰ X
              r6c8=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {[r3c9=r8c8], r5c9, r7c9}: [r3c9=r8c8], r5c9в‰ X в†’ r7c9=X
              box 9 {[r3c9=r8c8], r7c9, r9c7}: r7c9=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X
              r7c9=X в†’ r7c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r3c3, r7c3в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X and r9c2=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r3c9=r8c8]) = color(r6c6) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r8c8]}: [r3c9=r8c8]=X в†’ r3c3, r3c6в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r6c6=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
              col 9 {[r3c9=r8c8], r5c9, r7c9}: [r3c9=r8c8]=X в†’ r5c9, r7c9в‰ X
              box 9 {[r3c9=r8c8], r7c9, r9c7}: [r3c9=r8c8]=X в†’ r9c7в‰ X
              [r3c9=r8c8]=X в†’ r6c8, r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c6, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r3c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r7c3=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X and r9c2=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) в‰  color(r8c8). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c9, r8c8, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c8} and {r7c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c8) = X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X в†’ r3c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r8c8=X в†’ r7c9, r9c7в‰ X
              r8c8=X в†’ r3c9, r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c9) = color(r8c3) = X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r3c3, r7c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r7c9=X в†’ r3c9, r5c9в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              box 9 {r7c9, r8c8, r9c7}: r7c9=X в†’ r8c8, r9c7в‰ X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c7}: r9c2, r9c7в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r9c6} (spine [r3c3=r9c2]вЂ”r3c6).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c6], r7c9, r9c7, r8c8} (spine r7c9вЂ”r9c7).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c9=r8c8=r9c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r6c6, r6c8, r5c9, r7c9, r9c7} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_guardian] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r9c6} (spine [r3c3=r9c2]вЂ”r3c6).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c9, [r3c9=r9c6], r7c9, r9c7} (spine [r3c9=r9c6]вЂ”r7c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r9c7). Identify.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r9c7], r6c8, r6c6}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c9=r9c6], r6c8} and {[r5c9=r9c7], r6c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 3 {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6]}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ [r3c3=r9c2], r3c6в‰ X
        row 9 {[r3c3=r9c2], [r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r9c7]}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ [r5c9=r9c7]в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6], r6c6}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r9c7], r7c9}: [r3c9=r9c6]=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
        box 9 {[r5c9=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8}: [r5c9=r9c7], r7c9в‰ X в†’ r8c8=X
        r8c8=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        r6c8=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r5c9=r9c7]) = color(r6c6) = X
        row 9 {[r3c3=r9c2], [r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r9c7]}: [r5c9=r9c7]=X в†’ [r3c3=r9c2], [r3c9=r9c6]в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6], r6c6}: r6c6=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r9c7], r7c9}: [r5c9=r9c7]=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X
        box 9 {[r5c9=r9c7], r7c9, r8c8}: [r5c9=r9c7]=X в†’ r8c8в‰ X
        [r5c9=r9c7]=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 3 {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, [r3c9=r9c6]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        col 9 {[r3c6=r7c9], r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r3c6=r7c9]) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r7c9], r3c9}: [r3c6=r7c9]=X в†’ r3c3, r3c9в‰ X
        col 6 {[r3c6=r7c9], [r5c9=r6c6], r8c6}: [r3c6=r7c9]=X в†’ [r5c9=r6c6], r8c6в‰ X
        box 9 {[r3c6=r7c9], r9c7, r9c8}: [r3c6=r7c9]=X в†’ r9c7, r9c8в‰ X
        r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c7, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color([r5c9=r6c6]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6], r3c6, r8c6} (spine [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”r3c6).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6], [r3c9=r8c6], r7c9} (spine [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”[r3c9=r8c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r7c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c6=r7c9], [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6], [r3c9=r8c6], r8c3} (spine [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”[r3c9=r8c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c6=r7c9]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r7c9=r8c3], r9c2, r9c7, r9c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r3c6, r9c2, r9c8} = {[r5c9=r6c6], r7c9, r8c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r5c9=r6c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r7c9, r8c3, r8c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r7c9, r8c6:
      Case A: color(r7c9) = color(r8c6). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r6c7 guards oddagon {r6c7, r5c9, r3c9, r3c6, r6c6} (source [r7c9=r8c6]).
            в†’ color(r6c7) = color([r7c9=r8c6]). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c7=r7c9=r8c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r6c8, r6c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r7c9) в‰  color(r8c6). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r3c6, r8c6, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r3c3, r8c6} and {r3c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r8c6) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X
              r3c3=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c6=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r3c9, r7c9в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: r5c9=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c6, r6c7, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c6) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c3, r3c9в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r6c6, r8c6в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c8}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r9c8=X
              r9c8=X в†’ r6c8, r7c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c6, r6c7, r6c8}: r6c6, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r3c9, r7c9в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: r5c9=X and r6c7=X clash
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color(r3c3) = color([r5c9=r6c6]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r7c6, r9c4, r9c5, r9c2} (spine r9c4вЂ”r9c5).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r8c9, r8c3, [r7c6=r9c2]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 6, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r6c6, r9c2} = {r5c9, r7c6}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r3c6, r5c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r5c9). Identify.
        3.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r5c9], r6c6, r6c7, r6c8}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r5c9). Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: r8c3 guards oddagon {r8c3, r3c3, r3c6, r5c9, r8c9} (source r3c9).
            в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r3c9). Identify.
        5.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, [r3c9=r8c3], r9c2, r6c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r9c2} and {[r3c9=r8c3], r6c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r8c3]}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c3, [r3c9=r8c3]в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c5}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c4, r9c5в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r6c6, r7c6в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r9c4, r9c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r3c9=r8c3]) = color(r6c6) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r8c3]}: [r3c9=r8c3]=X в†’ r3c3, r3c6в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c6, r6c7, r6c8}: r6c6=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r6c6=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              col 9 {[r3c9=r8c3], r5c9, r8c9}: [r3c9=r8c3]=X в†’ r5c9, r8c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color([r3c9=r7c6]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  7.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c3=r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r7c6=r9c2], r8c3, r8c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r5c9, r6c6:
      Case A: color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r9c5 guards oddagon {r9c5, r7c6, r3c6, r3c9, r9c9} (source [r5c9=r6c6]).
            в†’ color(r9c5) = color([r5c9=r6c6]). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r8c5, r8c3, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {[r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r8c3} and {r8c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r5c9=r6c6=r9c5]) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 6 {[r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r6c7, r6c8}: [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5]=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X
              row 9 {[r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r9c2, r9c9}: [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5]=X в†’ r9c2, r9c9в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r7c6}: [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5]=X в†’ r3c6, r7c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r9c9}: [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5]=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X
              box 8 {[r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c5}: [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5]=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X
              r8c3=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 9 {[r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r9c2, r9c9}: r9c2=X в†’ [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r9c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r9c9}: [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r9c9в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              box 8 {[r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c5}: r8c5=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              r3c9=X в†’ r3c3, r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c5=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, [r5c9=r6c6=r9c5], r7c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r5c9) в‰  color(r6c6). Add edge.
        4.  Kв‚„ on {r5c9, r6c6, r6c7, r6c8}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel+guardian) ===

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_guardian] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c6, [r5c9=r6c6], r7c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r5c9=r6c6]).

      в†’ color(r8c5) = color(r9c9). Identify.

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c3) = color([r8c5=r9c9]) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r7c6]}: r3c3=X в†’ r3c6, [r3c9=r7c6]в‰ X
        row 9 {[r8c5=r9c9], r9c2, r9c5}: [r8c5=r9c9]=X в†’ r9c2, r9c5в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, [r3c9=r7c6], [r5c9=r6c6]}: r3c6, [r3c9=r7c6]в‰ X в†’ [r5c9=r6c6]=X

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r3c9=r7c6]) = color(r8c3) = X

        col 9 {[r3c9=r7c6], [r5c9=r6c6], [r8c5=r9c9]}: [r3c9=r7c6]=X в†’ [r8c5=r9c9]в‰ X

        r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 9 {[r8c5=r9c9], r9c2, r9c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

        col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r3c3=X в†’ r7c3в‰ X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6]} (spine r3c6вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color([r5c9=r6c6]). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6], r3c6, r9c6} (spine [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”r3c6).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6], [r3c9=r9c6], r8c9} (spine [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”[r3c9=r9c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r9c6], [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6], [r3c6=r8c9], r8c3} (spine [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”[r3c6=r8c9]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r9c6]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {[r3c6=r8c9], [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6], [r3c9=r8c3=r9c6], r7c3} (spine [r3c3=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”[r3c9=r8c3=r9c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c6=r8c9]) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  7.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r7c3=r8c9], [r3c9=r8c3=r9c6], r9c2, r9c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c3, r9c2:
      Case A: color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c9, r8c9, r9c8}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c3=r9c2], r8c9} and {r3c9, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c3=r9c2]) = color(r8c9) = X
              row 3 {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r3c9}: [r3c3=r9c2]=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 9 {[r3c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c8}: [r3c3=r9c2]=X в†’ r9c6, r9c8в‰ X
              col 3 {[r3c3=r9c2], r7c3, r8c3}: [r3c3=r9c2]=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r6c6=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X
              r6c6=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 3 {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ [r3c3=r9c2], r3c6в‰ X
              row 9 {[r3c3=r9c2], r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r6c6=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r5c9, r8c9в‰ X
              r6c6=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c3) в‰  color(r9c2). Add edge.
        3.  Kв‚„ on {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+hexagon) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.

      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
  3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c3, r3c9, [r5c9=r6c6], r9c6, r9c2, r7c3}
      Bridges: [r3c6=r8c9]вЂ”r9c8 (edges r3c3вЂ”r3c9 в†” r9c6вЂ”r9c2), [r3c6=r8c9]вЂ”r8c3 (edges r3c9вЂ”[r5c9=r6c6] в†” r9c2вЂ”r7c3), [r3c6=r8c9]вЂ”r8c3 (edges [r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”r9c6 в†” r7c3вЂ”r3c3).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of Cв‚† requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c3, r8c3, r9c2, r9c6} (source r9c3).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 5, col 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r3c9, r6c5} = {r6c8, r9c6}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c9, r5c9, r6c8, r6c5}:
      Diagonals: {r3c9, r6c8} and {r5c9, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c9) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c3], r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c3, [r3c6=r9c3]в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c5, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c5, r6c9в‰ X
        col 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c3], r8c3}: r3c3, [r3c6=r9c3]в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X
        box 7 {[r3c6=r9c3], r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
        r6c8=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        r8c3=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 9 {[r3c6=r9c3], r9c2, r9c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c9) = color(r6c5) = X
        row 6 {r6c5, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c8, r6c9в‰ X
        col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r6c5=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9}: r5c9=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c5, r8c5в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
        r9c6=X в†’ [r3c6=r9c3], r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c3], r3c9}: [r3c6=r9c3], r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c3=X
        col 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c3], r8c3}: r3c3=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X
        box 7 {[r3c6=r9c3], r8c3, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_set] (diamond+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5, r9c6} (spine r7c5вЂ”r8c5).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r6c8, [r6c5=r9c6]} (source r6c9).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c5=r9c6], r8c5, r8c3, r9c2}:
      Diagonals: {[r6c5=r9c6], r8c3} and {r8c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r6c5=r9c6]) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], [r6c5=r9c6], r6c8}: [r6c5=r9c6]=X в†’ [r3c6=r6c9], r6c8в‰ X
        row 9 {[r6c5=r9c6], r9c2, r9c3}: [r6c5=r9c6]=X в†’ r9c2, r9c3в‰ X
        col 3 {r3c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X
        col 5 {[r6c5=r9c6], r7c5, r8c5}: [r6c5=r9c6]=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
        box 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r5c9, r6c8}: [r3c6=r6c9], r6c8в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
        r5c9=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c5) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 9 {[r6c5=r9c6], r9c2, r9c3}: r9c2=X в†’ [r6c5=r9c6], r9c3в‰ X
        col 5 {[r6c5=r9c6], r7c5, r8c5}: r8c5=X в†’ r7c5в‰ X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r9c2=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X
        col 3 {r3c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r3c3=X
        r3c3=X в†’ [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], [r6c5=r9c6], r6c8}: [r3c6=r6c9], [r6c5=r9c6]в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
        col 9 {[r3c6=r6c9], r3c9, r5c9}: [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
        box 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r5c9, r6c8}: r5c9=X and r6c8=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c3, r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 3, col 5, col 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c9, r6c5} = {[r3c3=r9c2], r6c8, r9c6}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c3=r9c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c5, r6c8, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 3, col 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r3c3, [r3c9=r6c8]} = {[r3c9=r6c8], r9c2}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c8], r9c2, r9c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, r9c2} and {[r3c9=r6c8], r9c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r6c8]}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c3, [r3c9=r6c8]в‰ X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
        r3c6=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r3c9=r6c8]) = color(r9c6) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r6c8]}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X в†’ r3c3, r3c6в‰ X
        row 6 {[r3c9=r6c8], r6c5, r6c9}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X в†’ r6c5, r6c9в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c8], r5c9, r6c9}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
        [r3c9=r6c8]=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9, r6c8} (spine r5c9вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r5c9, [r3c9=r6c8], r6c9, r6c5} (spine [r3c9=r6c8]вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c5). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {[r3c9=r6c8], [r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r9c6} (spine [r3c3=r9c2]вЂ”r3c6).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r6c8]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r6c8=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c5], r7c5, r8c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/3 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c3, r7c5:
      Case A: color(r3c3) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c5, r9c6, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c6} and {r8c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              col 3 {[r3c3=r7c5], r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X в†’ [r3c3=r7c5], r7c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              box 8 {[r3c3=r7c5], r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X
              r9c6=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {[r3c3=r7c5], r3c6, r3c9}: [r3c3=r7c5], r3c6в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              col 5 {[r3c3=r7c5], r6c5, r8c5}: [r3c3=r7c5], r8c5в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r5c9, r6c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c8, r6c9}: r5c9, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
              r6c5=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              col 5 {[r3c3=r7c5], r6c5, r8c5}: r8c5=X в†’ [r3c3=r7c5], r6c5в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 8 {[r3c3=r7c5], r8c5, r9c6}: r8c5=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
              col 3 {[r3c3=r7c5], r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c3) в‰  color(r7c5). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c5, r9c6, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r9c6} and {r7c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X в†’ r3c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
              r9c6=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c6в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r7c5, r8c5в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r6c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r5c9, r6c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c8, r6c9}: r5c9, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
              r6c5=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r7c5=X в†’ r6c5, r8c5в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c5=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
              r7c5=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_set] (diamond+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5, r9c6} (spine r7c5вЂ”r8c5).
      в†’ color(r6c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r6c8, [r6c5=r9c6]} (source r6c9).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c5=r9c6], r7c5, r7c3, r9c2}:
      Diagonals: {[r6c5=r9c6], r7c3} and {r7c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r6c5=r9c6]) = color(r7c3) = X
        row 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], [r6c5=r9c6], r6c8}: [r6c5=r9c6]=X в†’ [r3c6=r6c9], r6c8в‰ X
        col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X в†’ r3c3, r8c3в‰ X
        col 5 {[r6c5=r9c6], r7c5, r8c5}: [r6c5=r9c6]=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
        box 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r5c9, r6c8}: [r3c6=r6c9], r6c8в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
        r5c9=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c5) = color(r9c2) = X
        col 5 {[r6c5=r9c6], r7c5, r8c5}: r7c5=X в†’ [r6c5=r9c6], r8c5в‰ X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
        col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r3c3=X
        r3c3=X в†’ [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], [r6c5=r9c6], r6c8}: [r3c6=r6c9], [r6c5=r9c6]в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
        col 9 {[r3c6=r6c9], r3c9, r5c9}: [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
        box 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r5c9, r6c8}: r5c9=X and r6c8=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 3, col 5, col 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c9, r6c5} = {[r3c3=r9c2], r6c8, r9c6}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c3=r9c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c5, r6c8, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c5} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c5). Identify.

  3.  Diamond {r3c6, r3c3, [r3c9=r8c5], r8c3} (spine r3c3вЂ”[r3c9=r8c5]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r8c3], r9c1, r9c2, r9c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c3, r6c5:
      Case A: color(r3c3) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c3=r6c5], r3c6, r9c6, r8c5}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c3=r6c5], r9c6} and {r3c6, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c3=r6c5]) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 3 {[r3c3=r6c5], r3c6, r3c9}: [r3c3=r6c5]=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 6 {[r3c3=r6c5], r6c7, r6c8}: [r3c3=r6c5]=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: r6c7, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              [r3c3=r6c5]=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r5c9=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              [r3c3=r6c5]=X and r8c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c6) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 3 {[r3c3=r6c5], r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ [r3c3=r6c5], r3c9в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r3c9, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: r5c9=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X
              r3c6=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r3c3=r6c5], r6c7, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c3) в‰  color(r6c5). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r6c5, r8c5, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r3c3, r8c5} and {r6c5, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c5=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r8c9}: r3c9, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: r5c9=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X
              r3c3=X в†’ r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c5=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c5, r6c7, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 6 {r6c5, r6c7, r6c8}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c9}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c5, r8c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: r6c7, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              r5c9=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c5=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c6}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
              r3c6=X and r9c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r7c5) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r6c5=r9c3], r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c3, [r3c6=r6c5=r9c3]в‰ X
              col 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r6c5=r9c3], r8c3}: r3c3, [r3c6=r6c5=r9c3]в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r5c9, r7c9в‰ X
              box 7 {[r3c6=r6c5=r9c3], r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              r7c5=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {[r3c6=r6c5=r9c3], r9c2, r9c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color([r3c6=r9c3]) в‰  color(r6c5). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r9c3], r6c5, r7c5, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r9c3], r7c5} and {r6c5, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r9c3]) = color(r7c5) = X

              row 9 {[r3c6=r9c3], r9c2, r9c6}: [r3c6=r9c3]=X в†’ r9c2, r9c6в‰ X

              [r3c6=r9c3]=X в†’ r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r7c5=X в†’ r7c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r5c9, r7c9}: r3c9, r7c9в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: r5c9=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c5, r6c7, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 6 {r6c5, r6c7, r6c8}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X
              row 9 {[r3c6=r9c3], r9c2, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ [r3c6=r9c3], r9c2в‰ X

              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8, r6c5} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r5c9) = color(r6c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, [r5c9=r6c5], r8c9, r8c5} (spine [r5c9=r6c5]вЂ”r8c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r8c5). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, r3c3, [r3c9=r8c5], r8c3} (spine r3c3вЂ”[r3c9=r8c5]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r8c3] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c3, [r3c9=r8c5], r9c6, r9c2, r7c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r9c6], [r5c9=r6c5], r7c5, r8c5}. Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c3, r7c5:
      Case A: color(r3c3) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c5, r9c6, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c6} and {r8c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              col 3 {[r3c3=r7c5], r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X в†’ [r3c3=r7c5], r7c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              box 8 {[r3c3=r7c5], r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X
              r9c6=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {[r3c3=r7c5], r3c6, r3c9}: [r3c3=r7c5], r3c6в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              col 5 {[r3c3=r7c5], r6c5, r8c5}: [r3c3=r7c5], r8c5в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              r3c9=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c5=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              col 5 {[r3c3=r7c5], r6c5, r8c5}: r8c5=X в†’ [r3c3=r7c5], r6c5в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 8 {[r3c3=r7c5], r8c5, r9c6}: r8c5=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
              col 3 {[r3c3=r7c5], r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c3) в‰  color(r7c5). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c5, r9c6, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r9c6} and {r7c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X в†’ r3c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
              r9c6=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c6в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r7c5, r8c5в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              r3c9=X в†’ r5c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c5=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r7c5=X в†’ r6c5, r8c5в‰ X

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of Cв‚† requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) в‰  color(r7c6). Add edge.
        3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c3, r3c9, r9c9, r9c5, r6c5, r6c3}

            Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
            but every 3-coloring of Cв‚† requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color(r3c3) = color([r6c9=r9c8]). Identify.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r3c3 guards oddagon {r3c3, r3c6, r7c6, r8c5, r8c3} (source r8c6).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r9c8} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color(r9c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  3.  SET: {col 6, col 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r3c6, [r7c6=r9c8]} = {r6c9, r8c5}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c3=r8c6], r3c9, r6c9, r8c5}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c3=r8c6], r6c9} and {r3c9, r8c5} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c3=r8c6]) = color(r6c9) = X
        row 3 {[r3c3=r8c6], r3c6, r3c9}: [r3c3=r8c6]=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
        row 8 {[r3c3=r8c6], r8c3, r8c5}: [r3c3=r8c6]=X в†’ r8c3, r8c5в‰ X
        col 6 {[r3c3=r8c6], r3c6, [r7c6=r9c8]}: [r3c3=r8c6]=X в†’ [r7c6=r9c8]в‰ X

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r8c5) = X

        row 8 {[r3c3=r8c6], r8c3, r8c5}: r8c5=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X

        Contradiction.

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r9c2:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c6=r9c2], r7c6, r8c4, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r9c2], r8c4} and {r7c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r9c2]) = color(r8c4) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c2], r3c9}: [r3c6=r9c2]=X в†’ r3c3, r3c9в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c4=X в†’ r8c3, r8c5в‰ X
              row 9 {[r3c6=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9}: [r3c6=r9c2]=X в†’ r9c8, r9c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r6c9=X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c4=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c6) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c4, r8c5в‰ X
              r7c6=X в†’ [r3c6=r9c2]в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c2], r3c9}: r3c3, [r3c6=r9c2]в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r6c9, r9c9в‰ X
              row 9 {[r3c6=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9}: [r3c6=r9c2], r9c9в‰ X в†’ r9c8=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r9c2). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r3c6, r9c2, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r3c3, r9c2} and {r3c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c8, r9c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r6c9=X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
              r3c3=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c6) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c3, r3c9в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c4, r8c5в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c4, r8c5в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              r3c6=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              r3c6=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c6, r8c6, r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {r7c3, r8c6} and {r7c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r8c6) = X
        col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X в†’ r3c3, r8c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X в†’ r3c6, r7c6в‰ X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c6, r9c5}: r8c6=X в†’ r9c5в‰ X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c6в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c2, r9c5в‰ X в†’ r9c8=X
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r5c8, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c9=X
        r3c9=X and r6c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r7c6) = color(r8c3) = X
        col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r3c3, r7c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6}: r7c6=X в†’ r3c6, r8c6в‰ X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c6, r8c6, r9c5}: r7c6=X в†’ r9c5в‰ X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c6в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c5, r9c8}: r9c2, r9c5в‰ X в†’ r9c8=X
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
        box 6 {r5c8, r6c8, r6c9}: r5c8, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c9=X
        r3c9=X and r6c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r9c8} (spine r5c8вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r6c9) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c3=r9c2], r3c9, [r6c9=r9c8]} (spine [r3c3=r9c2]вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color([r6c9=r9c8]). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r6c9=r9c8], r7c6, r8c6, r9c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 3, col 6, col 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r9c8} = {[r3c3=r9c2], r6c9, r9c5}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c3=r9c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, r9c5, r9c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  1.  Guardian: r8c6 guards oddagon {r8c6, r6c6, r6c9, r5c8, r8c8} (source r6c8).
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.

        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X

        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c6) = color(r8c3) = X

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (set+hexagon) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r9c2} = {r5c8}.
      в†’ color(r9c2) = color(r5c8). Identify.
  2.  Bridged hexagon: ring {r3c6, r3c9, r6c9, [r5c8=r9c2], r8c3, r8c6}
      Bridges: r3c3вЂ”r9c3 (edges r3c6вЂ”r3c9 в†” [r5c8=r9c2]вЂ”r8c3), r9c9вЂ”r8c8 (edges r3c9вЂ”r6c9 в†” r8c3вЂ”r8c6), r6c8вЂ”r6c6 (edges r6c9вЂ”[r5c8=r9c2] в†” r8c6вЂ”r3c6).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of Cв‚† requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+ladder+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c9} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Circular ladder {r6c6вЂ”r8c6, r6c8вЂ”r8c8, r6c9вЂ”[r8c3=r9c9]}:
      Satellites r3c6 (rung 1), r5c8 (rung 2), r3c9 (rung 3) forced to distinct colors. Add triangle.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub r3c9 forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c3, r3c6, r5c8, r6c9, [r8c3=r9c9]} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+hexagon) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 6, col 8, col 9}.
      Remainder: {[r3c3=r9c2], r8c3} = {r5c8, r9c9}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Bridged hexagon: ring {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r6c6, r6c8, r5c8, r9c9}
      Bridges: r3c9вЂ”r6c9 (edges [r3c3=r9c2]вЂ”r3c6 в†” r6c8вЂ”r5c8), r8c6вЂ”r8c8 (edges r3c6вЂ”r6c6 в†” r5c8вЂ”r9c9), r6c9вЂ”r3c9 (edges r6c6вЂ”r6c8 в†” r9c9вЂ”[r3c3=r9c2]).
      Each bridge forces opposite edges to miss different colors,
      but every 3-coloring of Cв‚† requires at least one matching pair. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_guardian] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r6c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r6c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r6c8, r6c6, [r6c9=r8c8], r8c6} (spine r6c6вЂ”[r6c9=r8c8]).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r3c6, [r6c8=r8c6], r8c3}:
      Diagonals: {r3c3, [r6c8=r8c6]} and {r3c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c3) = color([r6c8=r8c6]) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c6, [r6c8=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8]}: [r6c8=r8c6]=X в†’ r6c6, [r6c9=r8c8]в‰ X
        row 8 {[r6c8=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: [r6c8=r8c6]=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X
        col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r3c3=X в†’ r7c3в‰ X
        col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8]}: [r6c8=r8c6]=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r8c8], r9c9}: r3c9, [r6c9=r8c8]в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
        r9c2=X and r9c9=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c6) = color(r8c3) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c3, r3c9в‰ X
        row 8 {[r6c8=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8], r8c3}: r8c3=X в†’ [r6c8=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8]в‰ X
        col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r7c3в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, [r6c8=r8c6]}: r3c6=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X
        col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8]}: [r6c8=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8]в‰ X в†’ r5c8=X
        col 9 {r3c9, [r6c9=r8c8], r9c9}: r3c9, [r6c9=r8c8]в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c6, [r6c8=r8c6], [r6c9=r8c8]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

Proof of non-3-colorability:

      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r3c3 guards oddagon {r3c3, r3c6, [r5c8=r6c6], r8c8, r8c3} (source r8c6).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c3=r8c6], r3c9, r9c9, r8c8}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c3=r8c6], r9c9} and {r3c9, r8c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c3=r8c6]) = color(r9c9) = X

        row 8 {[r3c3=r8c6], r8c3, r8c8}: [r3c3=r8c6]=X в†’ r8c3, r8c8в‰ X

        col 6 {[r3c3=r8c6], r3c6, [r5c8=r6c6]}: [r3c3=r8c6]=X в†’ [r5c8=r6c6]в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r6c9в‰ X
        box 6 {[r5c8=r6c6], r6c7, r6c9}: [r5c8=r6c6], r6c9в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r8c8) = X
        row 3 {[r3c3=r8c6], r3c6, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ [r3c3=r8c6], r3c6в‰ X
        row 8 {[r3c3=r8c6], r8c3, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X

        [r5c8=r6c6]=X and r8c8=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9, r6c6} (spine r6c7вЂ”r6c9).
      в†’ color(r5c8) = color(r6c6). Identify.

      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c9, r3c3, [r3c6=r8c8], r8c3} (spine r3c3вЂ”[r3c6=r8c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r8c3], r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r6c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r6c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, [r3c6=r6c9], r8c6, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r3c3, r8c6} and {[r3c6=r6c9], r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r8c6) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9}: r3c3=X в†’ [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c6=X в†’ r8c3, r8c8в‰ X
              col 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c6, r8c6}: r8c6=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X
              col 9 {[r3c6=r6c9], r3c9, r9c9}: [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X
              r9c9=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c6, r6c7}: [r3c6=r6c9], r6c6в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
              box 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r5c8, r6c7}: r6c7=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color([r3c6=r6c9]) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r6c9], r3c9}: [r3c6=r6c9]=X в†’ r3c3, r3c9в‰ X
              row 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r6c6, r6c7}: [r3c6=r6c9]=X в†’ r6c6, r6c7в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c6, r8c8в‰ X
              col 9 {[r3c6=r6c9], r3c9, r9c9}: [r3c6=r6c9]=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X
              box 6 {[r3c6=r6c9], r5c8, r6c7}: [r3c6=r6c9]=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r6c9). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c9, r9c9, r8c8}:
            Diagonals: {r5c8, r9c9} and {r6c9, r8c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r3c9, r6c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: r5c8=X в†’ r6c7в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              r5c8=X в†’ r8c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X в†’ r3c3, r8c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
              row 6 {r6c6, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r6c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X and r6c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r8c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c6, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r6c6, r6c7в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6, r8c6в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r3c9, r9c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              r3c6=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              r3c6=X and r6c9=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c6, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c8, r6c7, [r6c6=r9c9], r7c8}:
            Diagonals: {r5c8, [r6c6=r9c9]} and {r6c7, r7c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r5c8) = color([r6c6=r9c9]) = X
              row 6 {[r6c6=r9c9], r6c7, r6c9}: [r6c6=r9c9]=X в†’ r6c7, r6c9в‰ X
              row 9 {[r6c6=r9c9], r9c1, r9c2}: [r6c6=r9c9]=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, [r6c6=r9c9], r7c6}: [r6c6=r9c9]=X в†’ r3c6, r7c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, [r6c6=r9c9], r6c9}: [r6c6=r9c9]=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              r5c8=X в†’ r7c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c7) = color(r7c8) = X
              row 6 {[r6c6=r9c9], r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7=X в†’ [r6c6=r9c9], r6c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, [r6c6=r9c9], r6c9}: [r6c6=r9c9], r6c9в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7=X в†’ r5c8в‰ X
              r3c9=X в†’ r3c3, r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r7c8=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, [r6c6=r9c9], r7c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c6) в‰  color(r9c9). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r6c6, r7c6, r7c8, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {r6c6, r7c8} and {r7c6, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r6c6) = color(r7c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c6, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c6=X в†’ r6c7, r6c9в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r6c6=X в†’ r3c6, r7c6в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, r6c9}: r6c7, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r5c8=X
              r6c6=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              r5c8=X and r7c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c6) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r7c6}: r7c6=X в†’ r3c6, r6c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r3c9, r6c9в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              r7c6=X в†’ r7c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+guardian) ===

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 8}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6, col 9}.
      Remainder: {r6c7, r8c8} = {r7c3, r9c9}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Guardian: r8c6 guards oddagon {r8c6, r6c6, r6c9, r5c8, r8c8} (source r6c7).
      в†’ color(r8c6) = color(r6c7). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c8, r9c9, r9c2}:

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===

      Case 1: color([r3c3=r8c6]) = color(r9c9) = X

        row 8 {[r3c3=r8c6], [r6c7=r8c8], r8c3}: [r3c3=r8c6]=X в†’ [r6c7=r8c8], r8c3в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
        col 6 {[r3c3=r8c6], r3c6, r6c6}: [r3c3=r8c6]=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c9) = color([r6c7=r8c8]) = X
        row 3 {[r3c3=r8c6], r3c6, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ [r3c3=r8c6], r3c6в‰ X
        row 6 {r6c6, [r6c7=r8c8], r6c8}: [r6c7=r8c8]=X в†’ r6c6, r6c8в‰ X
        row 8 {[r3c3=r8c6], [r6c7=r8c8], r8c3}: [r6c7=r8c8]=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X
        col 6 {[r3c3=r8c6], r3c6, r6c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r7c6 guards oddagon {r7c6, r6c6, r6c7, r5c8, r7c8} (source r6c8).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r3c9, r8c3:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r8c3). Identify.
        3.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3, r9c9}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) в‰  color(r8c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c9, r8c3, r9c2, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {r3c9, r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c9) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c3, r3c6в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c9}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c3, r9c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r3c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r3c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3=X and r9c2=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c2, r9c3в‰ X
              col 3 {r3c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X
              r8c3=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c9=X в†’ r7c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, [r6c8=r7c6]}: r3c6=X в†’ r6c6, [r6c8=r7c6]в‰ X
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r7c6], r7c8}: [r6c8=r7c6], r7c8в‰ X в†’ r5c8=X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, [r6c8=r7c6]}: r5c8=X в†’ r6c7в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c6, r6c7, [r6c8=r7c6]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r7c6 guards oddagon {r7c6, r6c6, r6c7, r5c8, r7c8} (source r6c8).
      в†’ color(r7c6) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Branch on r3c3, r7c8:
      Case A: color(r3c3) = color(r7c8). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r6c8=r7c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {[r3c3=r7c8], r3c6, r6c6, r6c7, r5c8} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c3) в‰  color(r7c8). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r3c9, r9c9, r7c8}:
            Diagonals: {r3c3, r9c9} and {r3c9, r7c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              r3c3=X в†’ r7c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r3c3=X and r8c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r7c8) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c3, r3c6в‰ X
              col 8 {r5c8, [r6c8=r7c6], r7c8}: r7c8=X в†’ r5c8, [r6c8=r7c6]в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c8, r6c7, [r6c8=r7c6]}: r5c8, [r6c8=r7c6]в‰ X в†’ r6c7=X
              r3c9=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              r6c7=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, [r6c8=r7c6]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  3.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r9c6} (spine [r3c3=r9c2]вЂ”r3c6).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c3=r9c2], [r3c9=r9c6], r9c5} (spine [r3c3=r9c2]вЂ”[r3c9=r9c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r9c6], r5c6, [r5c9=r6c5]}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c9, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  Guardian: r7c3 guards oddagon {r7c3, r3c3, r3c6, r9c6, r9c2} (source [r3c9=r9c5]).
            в†’ color(r7c3) = color([r3c9=r9c5]). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c9=r7c3=r9c5] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r3c3, r3c6, r9c6, r9c2, r8c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) в‰  color(r9c5). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r3c9, r5c9, r5c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r5c9} and {r3c9, r5c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r5c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c3, r3c9в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r5c6, r9c6в‰ X
              box 6 {r5c9, r6c7, r6c8}: r5c9=X в†’ r6c7, r6c8в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c5, r6c7, r6c8}: r6c7, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              r6c5=X в†’ r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c5, r9c6}: r9c5, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c9) = color(r5c6) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c3, r3c6в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
              r3c9=X в†’ r5c9, r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r5c6=X в†’ r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c5, r9c6}: r9c5, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r6c8], r8c8, r8c3, r9c2} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

            Diagonals: {r3c3, [r7c6=r9c8]} and {r3c6, r6c8} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color([r7c6=r9c8]) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 9 {[r7c6=r9c8], r9c1, r9c2}: [r7c6=r9c8]=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              box 8 {[r7c6=r9c8], r8c4, r8c5}: [r7c6=r9c8]=X в†’ r8c4, r8c5в‰ X
              r3c3=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r3c3=X and r8c3=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c6) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c3, r3c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
              r3c6=X в†’ [r7c6=r9c8]в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===

  1.  Branch on r6c6, r7c3:
      Case A: color(r6c6) = color(r7c3). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r6c6=r7c3], r6c8, r9c8, r9c2}:

              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).

        [r8c3=r9c5]=X and r8c6=X are adjacent
        Contradiction.

        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6=X в†’ r8c6в‰ X
        box 8 {r8c6, r9c4, r9c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r3c6) = color(r6c8) = X
        row 3 {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ [r3c3=r9c2], r3c9в‰ X
        col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r6c6, r8c6в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
        col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r6c8], r6c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r3c9=r6c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r6c8], [r3c3=r6c6], r3c6, r8c6} (spine [r3c3=r6c6]вЂ”r3c6).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r6c8]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r3c6, [r3c3=r6c6], [r3c9=r6c8=r8c6], r8c3} (spine [r3c3=r6c6]вЂ”[r3c9=r6c8=r8c6]).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c9=r6c8=r8c6], [r3c3=r6c6], [r3c6=r8c3], r7c3} (spine [r3c3=r6c6]вЂ”[r3c6=r8c3]).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r6c8=r8c6]) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r6c8=r7c3=r8c6], r9c2, r9c4, r9c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c9, r6c6:
      Case A: color(r3c9) = color(r6c6). Identify.
        2.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r6c6], r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c9) в‰  color(r6c6). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c9, r4c9, r6c8, r6c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c9, r6c8} and {r4c9, r6c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c9) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r3c3, r3c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
              r3c9=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r3c6, r6c6в‰ X в†’ r8c6=X
              box 8 {r8c6, r9c4, r9c5}: r8c6=X в†’ r9c4, r9c5в‰ X
              r8c6=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c4, r9c5}: r9c4, r9c5в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r3c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r7c3=X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X and r9c2=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r4c9) = color(r6c6) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r6c6, r8c6}: r6c6=X в†’ r3c6, r8c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9}: r4c9=X в†’ r3c9, r5c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r4c9=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c3=X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r3c3=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              r9c2=X в†’ r9c4, r9c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 8 {r8c6, r9c4, r9c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 3, col 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r3c3, [r3c9=r6c8]} = {[r3c9=r6c8], r9c2}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c8], r9c2, r9c6}:
      Diagonals: {r3c6, r9c2} and {[r3c9=r6c8], r9c6} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r6c8]}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c3, [r3c9=r6c8]в‰ X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
        r3c6=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color([r3c9=r6c8]) = color(r9c6) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r6c8]}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X в†’ r3c3, r3c6в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r6c8], r4c9, r5c9}: [r3c9=r6c8]=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
        box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
        [r3c9=r6c8]=X в†’ r6c5, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c9=r6c8], [r3c3=r9c2], r3c6, r9c6} (spine [r3c3=r9c2]вЂ”r3c6).
      в†’ color([r3c9=r6c8]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c9=r6c8=r9c6], r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c3, r7c5:
      Case A: color(r3c3) = color(r7c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r8c3, r8c5, r9c6, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r8c3, r9c6} and {r8c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r8c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              col 3 {[r3c3=r7c5], r7c3, r8c3}: r8c3=X в†’ [r3c3=r7c5], r7c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              box 8 {[r3c3=r7c5], r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r8c5в‰ X
              r9c6=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {[r3c3=r7c5], r3c6, r3c9}: [r3c3=r7c5], r3c6в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              col 5 {[r3c3=r7c5], r6c5, r8c5}: [r3c3=r7c5], r8c5в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r4c9, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
              r6c5=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              col 5 {[r3c3=r7c5], r6c5, r8c5}: r8c5=X в†’ [r3c3=r7c5], r6c5в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 8 {[r3c3=r7c5], r8c5, r9c6}: r8c5=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
              col 3 {[r3c3=r7c5], r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c3) в‰  color(r7c5). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r7c3, r7c5, r9c6, r9c2}:
            Diagonals: {r7c3, r9c6} and {r7c5, r9c2} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r7c3) = color(r9c6) = X
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3=X в†’ r3c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r7c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r7c5, r8c5в‰ X
              r9c6=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c6в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r7c5, r8c5в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: r4c9, r5c9в‰ X в†’ r6c8=X
              r6c5=X and r6c8=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c5) = color(r9c2) = X
              col 5 {r6c5, r7c5, r8c5}: r7c5=X в†’ r6c5, r8c5в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c5, r8c5, r9c6}: r7c5=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
              r7c5=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 3 {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c3, r7c3, r8c3, r9c2} (spine r7c3вЂ”r8c3).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  2.  SET: {col 3, col 5, col 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 6, box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {[r3c3=r9c2], r3c9, r6c5} = {[r3c3=r9c2], r6c8, r9c6}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c3=r9c2] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, r3c9, r6c5, r6c8, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r9c1, r9c2в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              r8c3=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c6в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r4c9, r5c9в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c9, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r6c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c3, r3c6, [r3c9=r6c8], r6c6} (spine r3c6вЂ”[r3c9=r6c8]).
      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2, r9c5} (spine r9c1вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color(r8c3) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c3=r6c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r3c6, [r3c9=r6c8], r6c5, [r8c3=r9c5], r7c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+set+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c9, r4c9, r5c9, r6c8} (spine r4c9вЂ”r5c9).

      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.

      Bivalue oddagon {r3c3, [r3c9=r6c8], r6c6, r7c6, r8c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/3 [no_ladder] (diamond+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c5). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c9, [r3c6=r9c5], r9c9, r9c2} (spine [r3c6=r9c5]вЂ”r9c9).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c9=r9c2], r8c3, r8c8, r9c9}:
      Diagonals: {[r3c9=r9c2], r8c8} and {r8c3, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color([r3c9=r9c2]) = color(r8c8) = X
        row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r9c2]}: [r3c9=r9c2]=X в†’ r3c3, [r3c6=r9c5]в‰ X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
        row 9 {[r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r9c2], r9c9}: [r3c9=r9c2]=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X
        col 6 {[r3c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c6}: [r3c6=r9c5], r8c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c2], r4c9, r9c9}: [r3c9=r9c2]=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c6, r8c8в‰ X
        row 9 {[r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r9c2], r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ [r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r9c2]в‰ X
        col 6 {[r3c6=r9c5], r7c6, r8c6}: [r3c6=r9c5], r8c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
        col 9 {[r3c9=r9c2], r4c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r4c9в‰ X
        r8c3=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c5], [r3c9=r9c2]}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 3/3 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8, r8c8} (spine r5c8вЂ”r6c8).
      в†’ color(r4c9) = color(r8c8). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6, r9c5} (spine r7c6вЂ”r8c6).

      в†’ color(r3c3) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  4.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c3=r9c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {[r3c6=r9c5], r3c9, [r4c9=r8c8], r8c3, r9c2} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c3, r7c6:
      Case A: color(r3c3) = color(r7c6). Identify.
        2.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c3=r7c6], r8c3, r8c5, r8c6}. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c3) в‰  color(r7c6). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r7c6, r8c5, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r3c3, r8c5} and {r7c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r8c5) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c6}: r8c5=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6}: r3c6, r8c6в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c5, r8c6}: r7c6=X and r8c5=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c6) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c5, r8c6}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c5, r8c6в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r7c6, r8c6}: r7c6=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X
              r7c6=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3, r3c6в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r4c9, r9c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c2, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r9c8=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў
