            Diagonals: {[r3c6=r9c2], r8c4} and {r7c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c6=r9c2]) = color(r8c4) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c2], r3c9}: [r3c6=r9c2]=X в†’ r3c3, r3c9в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c4=X в†’ r8c3, r8c5в‰ X
              row 9 {[r3c6=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9}: [r3c6=r9c2]=X в†’ r9c8, r9c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r4c9=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c4=X в†’ r7c6в‰ X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r7c6) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c4, r8c5в‰ X
              r7c6=X в†’ [r3c6=r9c2]в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X в†’ r3c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, [r3c6=r9c2], r3c9}: r3c3, [r3c6=r9c2]в‰ X в†’ r3c9=X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r9c9}: r3c9=X в†’ r4c9, r9c9в‰ X
              row 9 {[r3c6=r9c2], r9c8, r9c9}: [r3c6=r9c2], r9c9в‰ X в†’ r9c8=X
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r9c2). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c3, r3c6, r9c2, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r3c3, r9c2} and {r3c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c3) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c3=X в†’ r3c6, r3c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c8, r9c9}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c8, r9c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r3c9, r4c9, r9c9}: r3c9, r9c9в‰ X в†’ r4c9=X
              box 6 {r4c9, r5c8, r6c8}: r4c9=X в†’ r5c8, r6c8в‰ X
              r3c3=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 8 {r5c8, r6c8, r9c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c6) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c9}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c3, r3c9в‰ X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c3=X в†’ r8c4, r8c5в‰ X
              box 8 {r7c6, r8c4, r8c5}: r8c4, r8c5в‰ X в†’ r7c6=X
              r3c6=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              r3c6=X and r7c6=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 8, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 7, box 8}.
      Remainder: {r9c8} = {r7c6}.
      в†’ color(r9c8) = color(r7c6). Identify.
  2.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c3, r8c3, r9c2, [r7c6=r9c8]} (source r9c3).

  3.  Diamond {[r6c5=r8c8], r5c6, r8c6, r9c6} (spine r5c6вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color([r6c5=r8c8]) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r6c5=r8c8=r9c6], r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r2c9, r5c6:
      Case A: color(r2c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r2c9=r5c6], r3c8, r6c8, r6c5}:
            Diagonals: {[r2c9=r5c6], r6c8} and {r3c8, r6c5} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r2c9=r5c6]) = color(r6c8) = X
              row 6 {r6c5, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c8=X в†’ r6c5, r6c9в‰ X
              col 6 {[r2c9=r5c6], r8c6, r9c6}: [r2c9=r5c6]=X в†’ r8c6, r9c6в‰ X
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8=X в†’ r3c8, r8c8в‰ X
              col 9 {[r2c9=r5c6], r3c9, r6c9}: [r2c9=r5c6]=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X

              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c8) = color(r6c5) = X
              row 6 {r6c5, r6c8, r6c9}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c8, r6c9в‰ X
              col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r3c8=X в†’ r8c8в‰ X

        col 8 {r3c8, r6c8, r8c8}: r6c8, r8c8в‰ X в†’ r3c8=X
        box 3 {r2c9, r3c8, r3c9}: r3c8=X в†’ r2c9, r3c9в‰ X
        col 9 {r2c9, r3c9, r8c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===

      Case 1: color([r3c8=r6c9]) = color(r8c6) = X
        row 6 {[r3c8=r6c9], r6c4, r6c5}: [r3c8=r6c9]=X в†’ r6c4, r6c5в‰ X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c6=X в†’ r8c3, r8c8в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r8c6=X в†’ r5c6, r9c6в‰ X
        col 9 {r2c9, [r3c8=r6c9], r3c9}: [r3c8=r6c9]=X в†’ r2c9, r3c9в‰ X
        box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c6) = X
        row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c8}: r8c8=X в†’ r8c3, r8c6в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r8c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X
        box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c8, r2c9, r3c9, r6c9} (spine r2c9вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c8=r6c9], r6c4, r6c5, r5c6} (spine r6c4вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color([r3c8=r6c9]) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r8c8, [r3c8=r5c6=r6c9], r8c6, r9c6} (spine [r3c8=r5c6=r6c9]вЂ”r8c6).
      в†’ color(r8c8) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {r8c3, [r8c8=r9c6], r9c1, r9c2}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (xwing+branch) ===

  1.  Branch on r3c8, r9c1:
      Case A: color(r3c8) = color(r9c1). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c8=r9c1], r8c8, r8c6, r9c6}:

              box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X

            Case 2: color(r8c8) = color(r9c6) = X

      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c8]) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r5c6=r6c8=r8c3], r9c2, r9c3, r9c6}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 6, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 5, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r6c8, r9c6} = {r5c6, r8c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r3c8, r8c9:

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r3c8=r8c9], r6c8, r9c6, r8c6}:
            Diagonals: {[r3c8=r8c9], r9c6} and {r6c8, r8c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r3c8=r8c9]) = color(r9c6) = X

        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r5c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r6c6=X
        col 9 {r2c9, [r3c8=r5c9], r3c9}: [r3c8=r5c9]=X в†’ r2c9, r3c9в‰ X
        box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: r9c2=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
        r6c6=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c5) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 5 {[r3c8=r5c9], r5c5, r5c6}: r5c5=X в†’ [r3c8=r5c9], r5c6в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r9c2, r9c6в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r5c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r6c6=X
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c6}: r5c5=X and r6c6=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c8, r2c9, r3c9, r5c9} (spine r2c9вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r5c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r3c8=r5c9], r5c5, r5c6, r6c6} (spine r5c5вЂ”r5c6).
      в†’ color([r3c8=r5c9]) = color(r6c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r5c5, [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], r5c6, r9c6} (spine [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”r5c6).
      в†’ color(r5c5) = color(r9c6). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c6, [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], [r5c5=r9c6], r9c8} (spine [r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]вЂ”[r5c5=r9c6]).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r9c8). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c8=r5c9=r6c6], [r5c5=r9c6], [r5c6=r9c8], r9c2} (spine [r5c5=r9c6]вЂ”[r5c6=r9c8]).
      в†’ color([r3c8=r5c9=r6c6]) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  6.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r5c9=r6c6=r9c2], r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+xwing) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 3, col 6}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 5, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r6c3, r9c6} = {r5c5, r9c2}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  SET: {col 6, col 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 3, box 5}.
      Remainder: {r5c9, r9c6} = {r3c8, r5c5}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c8, r5c5, r9c2, r9c8}:
      Diagonals: {r3c8, r9c2} and {r5c5, r9c8} (non-adjacent).
      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
      Case 1: color(r3c8) = color(r9c2) = X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c6, r9c8в‰ X

        r3c8=X в†’ r5c5в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r7c3, r8c3в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
        col 9 {r2c9, r3c9, r5c9}: r2c9, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r5c9=X
        r5c9=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        r6c3=X в†’ r6c6в‰ X (adjacent)
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
        Contradiction.
      Case 2: color(r5c5) = color(r9c8) = X
        row 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r5c9}: r5c5=X в†’ r5c6, r5c9в‰ X
        row 9 {r9c2, r9c6, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ r9c2, r9c6в‰ X
        col 6 {r5c6, r6c6, r9c6}: r5c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r6c6=X
        box 5 {r5c5, r5c6, r6c6}: r5c5=X and r6c6=X clash
        Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

              box 3 {r2c9, r3c8, r3c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c3) = color(r9c8) = X

      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r5c6=r6c9]). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r8c9], [r3c8=r5c6=r6c9], [r8c3=r9c6], r8c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+oddwheel+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 6, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 5, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r6c9, r9c6} = {r5c6, r8c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r3c6, r8c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r8c9). Identify.
        3.  Odd wheel: hub [r3c6=r8c9] forces rim to 2 colors.
            Bivalue oddagon {r2c9, r3c8, r8c8, r8c3, r5c6, r9c6, r6c9} (length 7) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r8c9). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r8c9, r6c9, r9c6}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r6c9} and {r8c9, r9c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r6c9) = X
              row 6 {r6c4, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r6c4, r6c5в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r5c6, r9c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r2c9, r8c9в‰ X
              box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c9) = color(r9c6) = X
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c8, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r8c3, r8c8в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r9c6=X в†’ r3c6, r5c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r2c9, r6c9в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

              row 6 {r6c4, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c4=X в†’ r6c5, r6c8в‰ X
              box 5 {[r2c9=r5c6], r6c4, r6c5}: r6c4=X в†’ [r2c9=r5c6]в‰ X
              r3c8=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {[r2c9=r5c6], r3c6, r8c6}: [r2c9=r5c6], r3c6в‰ X в†’ r8c6=X
              r8c6=X в†’ r8c3, r8c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 9 {[r2c9=r5c6], r8c9, r9c9}: [r2c9=r5c6], r8c9в‰ X в†’ r9c9=X
              r9c9=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r2c9) в‰  color(r5c6). Add edge.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r2c9, r3c8, r3c6, r5c6}:
            Diagonals: {r2c9, r3c6} and {r3c8, r5c6} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r2c9) = color(r3c6) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r5c6, r8c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r8c9, r9c9}: r2c9=X в†’ r8c9, r9c9в‰ X
              r2c9=X в†’ r3c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 8 {r8c3, r8c6, r8c9}: r8c6, r8c9в‰ X в†’ r8c3=X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c8) = color(r5c6) = X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r3c6, r8c6в‰ X
              box 5 {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5}: r5c6=X в†’ r6c4, r6c5в‰ X

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_xwing] (diamond+oddwheel) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c6, r4c6, r5c6, r6c5} (spine r4c6вЂ”r5c6).

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+oddwheel+branch+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {col 3, col 6}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 5, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r3c6, r6c3} = {r6c5, r9c2}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r6c5, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r6c5) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r2c9, r3c8, r9c8, [r6c5=r9c9]}:
            Diagonals: {r2c9, r9c8} and {r3c8, [r6c5=r9c9]} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r2c9) = color(r9c8) = X
              row 9 {[r6c5=r9c9], r9c2, r9c8}: r9c8=X в†’ [r6c5=r9c9], r9c2в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, [r6c5=r9c9], r6c9}: r2c9=X в†’ r6c9в‰ X
              r2c9=X в†’ r3c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c9], r6c9}: [r6c5=r9c9], r6c9в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c8) = color([r6c5=r9c9]) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, [r6c5=r9c9], r6c9}: [r6c5=r9c9]=X в†’ r6c3, r6c9в‰ X
              row 9 {[r6c5=r9c9], r9c2, r9c8}: [r6c5=r9c9]=X в†’ r9c2, r9c8в‰ X

              r3c8=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              col 6 {r3c6, r4c6, r5c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r6c5) в‰  color(r9c9). Add edge.
        4.  Guardian: r7c3 guards oddagon {r7c3, r6c3, r6c9, r9c9, r9c2} (source r6c5).
            в†’ color(r7c3) = color(r6c5). Identify.

            Bivalue oddagon {r6c3, r6c9, r9c9, r9c2, r8c3} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  4.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r5c6=r8c3=r9c9], r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r3c8, r2c9, r3c9, r9c9} (spine r2c9вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r9c9). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r2c9, [r3c8=r9c9], r3c9, r3c6} (spine [r3c8=r9c9]вЂ”r3c9).
      в†’ color(r2c9) = color(r3c6). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3, r9c2} (spine r8c3вЂ”r9c3).
      в†’ color(r6c3) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r6c8, [r3c8=r9c9], [r6c3=r9c2], r9c3} (spine [r3c8=r9c9]вЂ”[r6c3=r9c2]).
      в†’ color(r6c8) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  5.  Diamond {[r3c8=r9c9], [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c8=r9c3], r6c5} (spine [r6c3=r9c2]вЂ”[r6c8=r9c3]).
      в†’ color([r3c8=r9c9]) = color(r6c5). Identify.
  6.  Diamond {r3c9, [r2c9=r3c6], [r3c8=r6c5=r9c9], r5c6} (spine [r2c9=r3c6]вЂ”[r3c8=r6c5=r9c9]).
      в†’ color(r3c9) = color(r5c6). Identify.
  7.  Diamond {[r3c8=r6c5=r9c9], [r2c9=r3c6], [r3c9=r5c6], r8c6} (spine [r2c9=r3c6]вЂ”[r3c9=r5c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c8=r6c5=r9c9]) = color(r8c6). Identify.
  8.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r6c5=r8c6=r9c9], [r6c3=r9c2], [r6c8=r9c3], r8c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 3, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r3c6, r9c9} = {r2c9, r8c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r3c6, r9c2:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r9c2). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r2c9, r8c3, r9c3, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {r2c9, r9c3} and {r8c3, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r2c9) = color(r9c3) = X
              row 9 {[r3c6=r9c2], r9c3, r9c9}: r9c3=X в†’ [r3c6=r9c2], r9c9в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r9c3=X в†’ r6c3, r8c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r3c9, r9c9}: r2c9=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X
              box 3 {r2c9, r3c8, r3c9}: r2c9=X в†’ r3c8в‰ X
              row 3 {[r3c6=r9c2], r3c8, r3c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {[r3c6=r9c2], r9c3, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ [r3c6=r9c2], r9c3в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r3c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r2c9, r3c9в‰ X
              box 3 {r2c9, r3c8, r3c9}: r2c9, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c8=X
              r3c8=X в†’ r6c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X в†’ r8c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              col 6 {[r3c6=r9c2], r5c6, r8c6}: [r3c6=r9c2], r8c6в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r9c2). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r2c9, r8c3, r9c2, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {r2c9, r9c2} and {r8c3, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r2c9) = color(r9c2) = X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c9}: r9c2=X в†’ r9c3, r9c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r3c9, r9c9}: r2c9=X в†’ r3c9в‰ X
              box 3 {r2c9, r3c8, r3c9}: r2c9=X в†’ r3c8в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r9c2=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X
              r9c2=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c8, r3c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r8c3) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c2, r9c3в‰ X
              col 3 {r6c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r6c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r3c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r2c9, r3c9в‰ X
              box 3 {r2c9, r3c8, r3c9}: r2c9, r3c9в‰ X в†’ r3c8=X

              r8c3=X в†’ r8c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c8}: r6c3, r6c8в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X

              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  3.  Diamond {[r2c9=r3c6], r5c6, r6c6, r6c5} (spine r5c6вЂ”r6c6).
      в†’ color([r2c9=r3c6]) = color(r6c5). Identify.

  7.  Kв‚„ on {[r2c9=r3c6=r6c5=r9c2], [r5c6=r6c3], r7c3, r8c3}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

      в†’ color(r2c9) = color(r3c6). Identify.
  2.  SET: {row 3, row 6, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 3, box 5, box 7}.
      Remainder: {[r2c9=r3c6], r6c9, r9c6} = {[r2c9=r3c6], r5c6, r8c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  3.  Odd wheel: hub [r2c9=r3c6] forces rim to 2 colors.
      Bivalue oddagon {r5c6, r8c3, r8c9, r6c9, r9c6} (length 5) cannot be 2-colored. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/1 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 3, box 7}.
      Remainder: {r3c6, r9c9} = {r2c9, r8c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r3c6, r6c5:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r6c5). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r2c9, r6c9, r6c3, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {r2c9, r6c3} and {r6c9, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r2c9) = color(r6c3) = X
              row 6 {[r3c6=r6c5], r6c3, r6c9}: r6c3=X в†’ [r3c6=r6c5], r6c9в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r2c9=X в†’ r9c9в‰ X
              box 3 {r2c9, r3c7, r3c8}: r2c9=X в†’ r3c7, r3c8в‰ X
              r2c9=X в†’ r8c3в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {[r3c6=r6c5], r3c7, r3c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c9) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 6 {[r3c6=r6c5], r6c3, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ [r3c6=r6c5], r6c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r2c9, r9c9в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c1, r9c2}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X
              r8c3=X в†’ r8c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r3c6) в‰  color(r6c5). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r6c5, r6c9, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r6c9} and {r6c5, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r6c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c7, r3c8}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c7, r3c8в‰ X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r6c3, r6c5в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r8c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r5c6, r8c6в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r6c9, r9c9}: r6c9=X в†’ r2c9, r9c9в‰ X
              box 3 {r2c9, r3c7, r3c8}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r6c5) = color(r9c9) = X
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c5=X в†’ r6c3, r6c9в‰ X
              row 9 {r9c1, r9c2, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r9c1, r9c2в‰ X

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r3c6, r9c9:
      Case A: color(r3c6) = color(r9c9). Identify.
        2.  Kв‚„ on {r2c9, [r3c6=r9c9], r3c7, r3c8}. Contradiction.

        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r3c6, r5c6, r5c9, r9c9}:
            Diagonals: {r3c6, r5c9} and {r5c6, r9c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r3c6) = color(r5c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c6, r3c7, r3c8}: r3c6=X в†’ r3c7, r3c8в‰ X

              col 9 {r2c9, r5c9, r9c9}: r9c9=X в†’ r2c9, r5c9в‰ X

              r5c6=X в†’ r3c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c9=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c3, r6c4, r6c5}: r6c4, r6c5в‰ X в†’ r6c3=X
              col 3 {r6c3, r7c3, r8c3}: r6c3=X в†’ r7c3, r8c3в‰ X
              box 7 {r7c3, r8c3, r9c2}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Branch on r6c1, r9c5:
      Case A: color(r6c1) = color(r9c5). Identify.
        2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r5c5, r5c9, r6c9, [r6c1=r9c5]}:

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 6}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{box 3, box 4}.
      Remainder: {r3c6, r6c9} = {r2c9, r5c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r2c9, r3c7, r3c6, r6c9}:

      By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.

  6.  Diamond {[r3c6=r9c3], [r3c3=r9c2], [r3c8=r6c9=r8c3=r9c6], r6c2} (spine [r3c3=r9c2]вЂ”[r3c8=r6c9=r8c3=r9c6]).
      в†’ color([r3c6=r9c3]) = color(r6c2). Identify.
  7.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c6=r6c2=r9c3], [r3c8=r6c9=r8c3=r9c6], r5c6, r6c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 1/2 [no_diamond] (set+xwing+branch) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  SET: {row 3, row 9}вЂ‰в€’вЂ‰{col 3, col 6}.
      Remainder: {r3c8, r9c2} = {r5c6, r8c3}.
      в†’ Both sides forced all-distinct. Virtual edges added.
  2.  Branch on r2c9, r3c3:
      Case A: color(r2c9) = color(r3c3). Identify.
        3.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {[r2c9=r3c3], r3c6, r5c6, r8c3}:
            Diagonals: {[r2c9=r3c3], r5c6} and {r3c6, r8c3} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color([r2c9=r3c3]) = color(r5c6) = X
              row 3 {[r2c9=r3c3], r3c6, r3c8}: [r2c9=r3c3]=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
              col 3 {[r2c9=r3c3], r8c3, r9c3}: [r2c9=r3c3]=X в†’ r8c3, r9c3в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r5c6=X в†’ r9c6в‰ X
              col 9 {[r2c9=r3c3], r6c9, r8c9}: [r2c9=r3c3]=X в†’ r6c9, r8c9в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              r5c6=X в†’ r6c5в‰ X (adjacent)
              r9c2=X в†’ r6c2в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c9}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c6) = color(r8c3) = X
              row 3 {[r2c9=r3c3], r3c6, r3c8}: r3c6=X в†’ [r2c9=r3c3], r3c8в‰ X
              col 3 {[r2c9=r3c3], r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c3в‰ X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X в†’ r5c6, r9c6в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              r8c3=X в†’ r8c9в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c6}: all в‰ X, but one must be X
              Contradiction.
      Case B: color(r2c9) в‰  color(r3c3). Add edge.
        4.  Pigeonhole X-wing on {r2c9, r3c3, r8c3, r8c9}:
            Diagonals: {r2c9, r8c3} and {r3c3, r8c9} (non-adjacent).
            By pigeonhole, one diagonal must share a color.
            Case 1: color(r2c9) = color(r8c3) = X
              col 3 {r3c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r3c3, r9c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r2c9=X в†’ r6c9, r8c9в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3=X в†’ r9c2в‰ X
              r2c9=X в†’ r3c8в‰ X (adjacent)
              r8c3=X в†’ r5c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c8}: r3c3, r3c8в‰ X в†’ r3c6=X
              row 9 {r9c2, r9c3, r9c6}: r9c2, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r9c6=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6=X and r9c6=X clash
              Contradiction.
            Case 2: color(r3c3) = color(r8c9) = X
              row 3 {r3c3, r3c6, r3c8}: r3c3=X в†’ r3c6, r3c8в‰ X
              col 3 {r3c3, r8c3, r9c3}: r3c3=X в†’ r8c3, r9c3в‰ X
              col 9 {r2c9, r6c9, r8c9}: r8c9=X в†’ r2c9, r6c9в‰ X
              box 7 {r8c3, r9c2, r9c3}: r8c3, r9c3в‰ X в†’ r9c2=X
              r9c2=X в†’ r6c2, r9c6в‰ X (adjacent)
              row 6 {r6c2, r6c5, r6c9}: r6c2, r6c9в‰ X в†’ r6c5=X
              col 6 {r3c6, r5c6, r9c6}: r3c6, r9c6в‰ X в†’ r5c6=X
              r5c6=X and r6c5=X are adjacent
              Contradiction.
      Both cases contradict 3-colorability.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Alt proof 2/2 [no_oddwheel] (diamond+guardian) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Guardian: r3c6 guards oddagon {r3c6, r3c3, r8c3, r9c2, r9c6} (source r9c3).
      в†’ color(r3c6) = color(r9c3). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c3, [r3c6=r9c3], r8c3} (spine r3c3вЂ”[r3c6=r9c3]).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color(r8c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {[r3c8=r8c3], r2c9, r8c9, r6c9} (spine r2c9вЂ”r8c9).
      в†’ color([r3c8=r8c3]) = color(r6c9). Identify.
  4.  Diamond {r5c6, [r3c6=r9c3], r9c6, r9c2} (spine [r3c6=r9c3]вЂ”r9c6).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r9c2). Identify.
  5.  Kв‚„ on {[r3c8=r6c9=r8c3], [r5c6=r9c2], r6c2, r6c5}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў

=== Greedy proof (diamond) ===
Proof of non-3-colorability:
  Assume for contradiction it is 3-colorable.

  1.  Diamond {r5c6, r6c4, r6c5, r6c2} (spine r6c4вЂ”r6c5).
      в†’ color(r5c6) = color(r6c2). Identify.
  2.  Diamond {[r5c6=r6c2], r8c2, r9c2, r7c3} (spine r8c2вЂ”r9c2).
      в†’ color([r5c6=r6c2]) = color(r7c3). Identify.
  3.  Diamond {r3c8, r3c3, r3c6, [r5c6=r6c2=r7c3]} (spine r3c3вЂ”r3c6).
      в†’ color(r3c8) = color([r5c6=r6c2=r7c3]). Identify.
  4.  Kв‚„ on {r2c9, [r3c8=r5c6=r6c2=r7c3], r5c9, r7c9}. Contradiction.

  Therefore the pattern is not 3-colorable. в–Ў
